blocks:
Ayahs

ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنزَلَ عَلَىٰ عَبْدِهِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَلَمْ يَجْعَل لَّهُۥ عِوَجَا ۜ (۱)
All praise belongs to Allah, who has sent down the Book to His servant and did not let any crookedness be in it, (1)
قَيِّمًا لِّيُنذِرَ بَأْسًا شَدِيدًا مِّن لَّدُنْهُ وَيُبَشِّرَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ أَنَّ لَهُمْ أَجْرًا حَسَنًا (۲)
[a Book] upright, to warn of a severe punishment from Him, and to give good news to the faithful who do righteous deeds, that there shall be for them a good reward, (2)

Key Points

Among the Surahs of the Quran, Al-Hamd, Al-An'am, Saba, Fatir, and Al-Kahf begin with the phrase "Alhamdulillah" (All praise belongs to Allah). In three of these Surahs, the discussion concerns the creation of existence. In Surah Al-Hamd, the discussion is about nurturing (Tarbiyah), and here (in Al-Kahf), it is about the Divine Book. It seems that existence and creation, along with the Book and the Law, are two wings for the nurturing of human beings. I‘wijāj (اعوجاج) means deviation and crookedness. ‘Awaj (عَوَج) is used for tangible things, and ‘iwaj (عِوَج) is used for intangible things. (3) And Qayyim (قیّم) means the Establisher and the Organizer. The previous Surah (Al-Isra) began with "Subḥāna Alladhī" (Glory be to Him Who), and this Surah begins with "Alhamdulillah." Glorification (Tasbīḥ) and praise (Taḥmīd) are usually found side by side. The word ‘Abduh (His servant) in the Quran, wherever it appears absolutely (without qualification), refers to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, such as "anẓala al-Furqāna ‘alā ‘Abdihi" (He sent down the Criterion upon His servant) (4), "fa-awḥā ilā ‘Abdihi" (and He revealed to His servant) (5), "yunazzilu ‘alā ‘Abdihi" (He sends down upon His servant) (6), "a-laysa Allāhu bi-kāfin ‘Abdahū" (Is not Allah sufficient for His servant?) (7).

3) ‘Awaj (عَوَج) is for human beings, and ‘iwaj (عِوَج) is for non-human things. Tafsir Tibyan. 4) Al-Furqan, 1. 5) An-Najm, 10. 6) Al-Hadid, 9. 7) Az-Zumar, 36.

Messages

1- The blessing of the Book and the Law is so important that Allah has praised Himself because of it. "Alhamdulillah Alladhī anzala..."

2- Servitude to Allah is the prerequisite for receiving spiritual perfections. "anzala ‘alā ‘Abdihi"

3- There is no deviation (I‘wijāj) in the Book of Allah, nor in His Messenger; it does not turn from truth to falsehood, nor from beneficial to non-beneficial, nor from sound to corrupt. "lam yaj‘al lahu ‘iwajan"

4- The Quran is steadfast and non-abrogable until the Day of Judgment. "Qayyiman"

5- The Quran is the guardian of the Divine Books and encompasses all the interests (Maṣāliḥ) of the servants. There is no contradiction, disagreement, excess, or deficiency in it; it both calls for rising up (Qiyām) and rises up to the call. "Qayyiman"

6- The condition for establishing the interests of others is that the reformer (Muṣliḥ) must be free from deviation (I‘wijāj). "lam yaj‘al lahu ‘iwajan, Qayyiman"

7- Averting harm is more important than attracting benefit. Therefore, warning (Indhār) precedes good tidings (Bishārah). "li-yundhira, wa yu-bashshira"

8- Faith accompanied by righteous deeds is effective. "al-mu’minīna, alladhīna ya‘malūna al-ṣāliḥāti"

9- Righteous deeds must be continuous. "ya‘malūna"

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Kahf

This Surah has 110 verses, and except for verse 28, all of it was revealed in Mecca. This Surah discusses the story of the Companions of the Cave (Aṣḥāb al-Kahf), the story of Moses and Khidr, the story of Dhul-Qarnayn (1), and issues concerning the Origin (Mabda’) and the Return (Ma‘ād). The polytheists of Quraysh sent people from Mecca to Medina to inquire about the mission of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله and the sign of His truthfulness from the Jewish scholars. The Jewish scholars told the envoys of the polytheists: Ask him three things; if He answers two and leaves one unanswered, He is truthful. Ask about the Companions of the Cave, Dhul-Qarnayn, and the Spirit (Rūḥ). If He answers concerning the Companions of the Cave and Dhul-Qarnayn, but does not answer concerning the Spirit, then He is a Prophet. (2) There are many narrations regarding the virtue of this Surah and the effects of its recitation, a few examples of which are mentioned here: Whoever recites this Surah is protected from the trial (Fitnah) of the Dajjal (Antichrist). Its recitation on the night of Friday causes the forgiveness of sins until the following Friday and three days thereafter. Reciting its last verse causes one to wake up at any hour of the night one desires. The recitation of this Surah prevents the torment of Hellfire and prevents Satan from entering the house in which this Surah has been recited that night. In any case, reading and recitation accompanied by understanding and acting upon it is the key to attaining the blessings of the Quran.

1) These three stories are mentioned only in this Surah. 2) Tafsir Durr al-Manthūr.

Ayahs

مَّـٰكِثِينَ فِيهِ أَبَدًا (۳)
to abide in it forever, (3)
وَيُنذِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوا۟ ٱتَّخَذَ ٱللَّهُ وَلَدًا (٤)
and to warn those who say, ‘Allah has taken a son.’ (4)

Key Points

Although the warning was mentioned in the previous Ayah, the admonition is repeated due to the importance of the deviation of the polytheists regarding attributing a son to Allah. Considering other verses of the Quran, the accusation of Allah having a son was a great and widespread slander among the polytheists. Christians also spoke of "أب، اِبن و روح القدس" (Father, Son, and Holy Spirit). Jews also considered ‘Uzayr (Ezra) to be the son of Allah, and the polytheists believed the angels were the daughters of Allah. Whereas this belief is neither consistent with reality nor compatible with reason.

Messages

1- Continuous action results in continuous reward. In the previous Ayah, "یعملون الصالحات" (they do righteous deeds) indicated the continuity of the action, and in this Ayah, "ماكثین" (abiding) means continuous reward.

2- Man desires eternal and lasting life, and Allah has placed that in the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah). "ماكثین فیه ابدا" (abiding therein forever).

3- One of the characteristics of divine blessings in the Hereafter is their permanence; there is no death or annihilation on the Day of Judgment. "ماكثین فیه ابداً" (abiding therein forever).

4- Correcting people's beliefs is among the duties of the prophets. "ینذر الّذین قالوا" (He warns those who say).

5- Ignorance and lack of awareness are the foundation of deviation. "قالوا... مالهم به من علم" (They say... they have no knowledge of it).

6- Beliefs must be based on knowledge, and belief without knowledge is criticized. "ما لهم به من علم" (They have no knowledge of it).

7- Whoever lacks faith also slanders his Creator. "كَبُرت كلمة..." (A grievous word has come forth...).

8- Attributing a son to Allah is a baseless slander. "ما لهم به من علم" (They have no knowledge of it).

9- Attributing a son to Allah is polytheism (shirk), an inappropriate comparison (tashbīh), and implies that Allah is in need of a successor or partner. "كَبُرت..." (A grievous word...).

Ayah

مَّا لَهُم بِهِۦ مِنْ عِلْمٍ وَلَا لِـَٔابَآئِهِمْ ۚ كَبُرَتْ كَلِمَةً تَخْرُجُ مِنْ أَفْوَٰهِهِمْ ۚ إِن يَقُولُونَ إِلَّا كَذِبًا (٥)
They do not have any knowledge of that, nor did their fathers. Monstrous is the utterance that comes out of their mouths, and they say nothing but a lie. (5)

Key Points

Although the warning was mentioned in the previous Ayah, the admonition is repeated due to the importance of the deviation of the polytheists regarding Allah taking a son. Considering other verses of the Quran, the accusation that Allah has a son was a great and widespread slander among the polytheists. Christians also spoke of "Father, Son, and Holy Spirit." Jews also considered 'Uzayr (عُزیر) to be the son of God, and the polytheists believed the angels were the daughters of God. While this belief is neither consistent with reality nor compatible with reason.

Messages

1- Continuous action yields continuous reward. In the previous Ayah, "یعملون الصالحات" (they do righteous deeds) was a sign of the continuity of the action, and in this Ayah, "ماكثین" (abiding) means continuous reward.

2- Man desires eternal and lasting life, and Allah has placed that in the Hereafter. "ماكثین فیه ابدا" (abiding therein forever).

3- One of the characteristics of divine blessings in the Hereafter is their permanence; there is no death or annihilation in the Resurrection. "ماكثین فیه ابداً" (abiding therein forever).

4- Correcting people's beliefs is among the duties of the prophets. "ینذر الّذین قالوا" (that He may warn those who say).

5- Ignorance and lack of awareness are the foundation of deviation. "قالوا... مالهم به من علم" (they say... they have no knowledge of it).

6- Beliefs must be based on knowledge, and belief without knowledge is criticized. "ما لهم به من علم" (they have no knowledge of it).

7- Whoever lacks faith also slanders his Creator. "كَبُرت كلمة..." (Grievous is the word...).

8- Allah having a son is a baseless slander. "ما لهم به من علم" (they have no knowledge of it).

9- Attributing a son to Allah is polytheism (shirk), an inappropriate comparison (tashbīh), and implies that Allah is in need of a successor or partner. "كَبُرت..." (Grievous is...).

Ayah

فَلَعَلَّكَ بَـٰخِعٌ نَّفْسَكَ عَلَىٰٓ ءَاثَـٰرِهِمْ إِن لَّمْ يُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِهَـٰذَا ٱلْحَدِيثِ أَسَفًا (٦)
You are liable to imperil your life out of grief for their sake, if they should not believe this discourse. (6)

Key Points

Asaf (أَسَف) is a stage more intense than huzn (grief/sorrow). The expression, «لعلّك باخع نفسك على آثارهم» (Perhaps you will destroy yourself, following after them), conveys the ultimate compassion of the Prophet. It is as if the Prophet is likened to a person who sees his dearest ones separating from him, and he looks at them with regret and longing as they depart.

Messages

1- Compassion and sorrow over the misguidance of others is a virtue, and the Prophet is the most compassionate of people. «باخعٌ نفسك»

2- The leader must be concerned with reforming the beliefs and actions of the people and must pursue this persistently. «على آثارهم»

3- The Quran contains fresh words and a new message. Hadith (حدیث). The preacher (propagator) must also possess innovation. (Hadith means new and fresh.)

Ayahs

إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا مَا عَلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ زِينَةً لَّهَا لِنَبْلُوَهُمْ أَيُّهُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا (۷)
Indeed We have made whatever is on the earth an adornment for it that We may test them [to see] which of them is best in conduct. (7)
وَإِنَّا لَجَـٰعِلُونَ مَا عَلَيْهَا صَعِيدًا جُرُزًا (۸)
And indeed We will turn whatever is on it into a barren plain. (8)

Key Points

Sa'īd (صَعِيد) refers to soil and the entire surface of the earth. Juruz (جُرُز) is barren land without vegetation. Allah placed the word لِنَبْلُوَهُمْ (related to the issue of testing people) between the two words جَعَلْنَا (which relates to the greenery of the earth) and جَاعِلُونَ (which relates to its barrenness). This indicates that the trial and test of people occur amidst greenery and vitality, as well as dryness and sorrow. However, what is important is the performance of man and his success. In narrations, the meaning of أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا (best in deeds) is work that is wise, accompanied by piety, and stored up for the Day of Judgment as well.(8)

8) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 5, p. 361 and Vol. 3, p. 243.

Messages

1- The reason for the disbelievers' lack of faith is their intoxication with the allurements of the world. لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا... جَعَلْنَا مَا عَلَى الْأَرْضِ زِينَةً

2- Everything on the earth—gardens, flowers, fruits, animals, waters, minerals, colors, scents—is the adornment of the earth. زِينَةً لَهَا. However, for developed human beings, faith is the adornment and beauty.(9)

3- Adornments are a means of testing. زِينَةً لَهَا لِنَبْلُوَهُمْ, so that it becomes known who is deceived and sells himself, and who, through contentment, asceticism (zuhd), and noble conduct, makes them a prelude to righteous deeds.

4- Testing is a sign of human free will.(10) لِيَبْلُوَهُمْ

5- The quality of the deed is important, not its quantity or amount. أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا, not أَكْثَرُ عَمَلًا.

6- The beauty of flowers and nature is transient, but good deeds are lasting. أَيُّكُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا... صَعِيدًا جُرُزًا

7- Let us not be deceived by the apparent beauties of the world, for they will all be leveled with the dust. صَعِيدًا جُرُزًا

9) حَبَّبَ إِلَيْكُمُ الْإِيمَانَ وَزَيَّنَهُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ. (Al-Hujurat, 7).

10) Testing is used in cases where man has the power of choice.

Ayah

أَمْ حَسِبْتَ أَنَّ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْكَهْفِ وَٱلرَّقِيمِ كَانُوا۟ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا عَجَبًا (۹)
Do you suppose that the Companions of the Cave and the Inscription were among Our wonderful signs? (9)

Key Points

«رَقِیم» means a writing and a tablet upon which the story of the Companions of the Cave was written and their names were inscribed. «كَهف» refers to a large cave.

The Ayah says: More important than putting a few people to sleep in the cave and then waking them up, is the creation of numerous and abundant beauties on the earth and then erasing them. This is because the incident of the Companions of the Cave is not very astonishing compared to the Divine power.

The incident of the Companions of the Cave occurred after the time of Prophet Jesus and before the advent of the Prophet of Islam, in the land of Rome and the city of Ephesus, and their tyrant was named Decius. The lesson that can be learned from this incident is the lesson of migration, steadfastness, and Divine aid, and one should not be concerned with the place, time, number, etc.

Apparently, the Companions of the Cave and the Raqīm refer to one group. And some have considered Raqīm to be the name of the region of the Companions of the Cave. Although in some exegeses, the Companions of the Raqīm are considered to be the three people who left their homes for work and life, were caught by rain on the way, and took refuge in a cave. With the falling of a rock piece from the mountain, the entrance of the cave was closed, and they became imprisoned. Each of them recalled a good and sincere deed they had performed and asked Allah to save them because of that deed. Each time, the rock moved slightly, until finally they were saved. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, after narrating this story, said: Whoever is with Allah will be saved. (11)

(11) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan, Nur al-Thaqalayn, Kashf al-Asrar, Durr al-Manthur.

Ayahs

إِذْ أَوَى ٱلْفِتْيَةُ إِلَى ٱلْكَهْفِ فَقَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةً وَهَيِّئْ لَنَا مِنْ أَمْرِنَا رَشَدًا (۱٠)
When the youths took refuge in the Cave, they said, ‘Our Lord! Grant us a mercy from Yourself, and help us on to rectitude in our affair.’ (10)
فَضَرَبْنَا عَلَىٰٓ ءَاذَانِهِمْ فِى ٱلْكَهْفِ سِنِينَ عَدَدًا (۱۱)
So We put them to sleep in the Cave for several years. (11)

Key Points

Fityah (فِتیَة) is the plural of fatā (فَتى), meaning young man or gallant/chivalrous man. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Fatā is used for a person of faith, because Allah introduced them as fatā even though they were elderly, due to their faith.(12) Rashad (رَشَد) means maturity (rushd), salvation, and divine pleasure.(13) The word rushd is used three times in this Surah: in the story of the Companions of the Cave, the story of Moses and Khidr (Ayah 66), and the Prophet of Islam (Ayah 23). It is mentioned in a tradition that the meaning of "ضَرَبنا فى آذانهم" (We sealed their ears) is the sealing of their ears, their sleep of 309 years, and their subsequent revival.(14)

12) كانوا شیوخاً فسمّاهم اللّه عزّوجلّ فتیة بایمانهم» كافى، ج ۸، ص ۳۹۸ ؛ تفسیر نورالثقلین. 13) تفسیر مجمع البیان. 14) تفسیر نورالثقلین.

Messages

1- To preserve religion, chivalry (young manhood), sacrificing comfort and ease, and migration are necessary. "اَوَى الفِتیَة الى الكهف"

2- Supplication (Du'a) accompanied by effort and action is effective. "اَوَى... ربّنا"

3- Migration (Hijrah) from a corrupt environment to preserve faith and values is a chivalrous act. "اَوَى الفِتیَة الى الكهف"

4- Migration and rising up for Allah pave the way for receiving divine aid. "اَوَى الفِتیة الى الكهف فضربنا"

Ayah

ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَـٰهُمْ لِنَعْلَمَ أَىُّ ٱلْحِزْبَيْنِ أَحْصَىٰ لِمَا لَبِثُوٓا۟ أَمَدًا (۱۲)
Then We aroused them that We might know which of the two groups better reckoned the period they had stayed. (12)

Key Points

The question of which group counts more accurately perhaps refers to Ayah 19 of this very Surah, where the Companions of the Cave disagreed regarding the duration of their sleep and stay. «قال قائل منهم كم لبثتم...»

Allah knows everything from the beginning and is All-Knowing. Therefore, the intent of «لنعلم» (that We may know) is either that Allah has spoken in the language of the people, or that He has actualized and brought into reality that which He already knows.

Ayah

نَّحْنُ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ نَبَأَهُم بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ فِتْيَةٌ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِرَبِّهِمْ وَزِدْنَـٰهُمْ هُدًى (۱۳)
We relate to you their account in truth. They were indeed youths who had faith in their Lord, and We had enhanced them in guidance, (13)

Key Points

People fall into three categories regarding corruption and a corrupt society: A: A group that is absorbed into the corruption of society. (Those who do not migrate and do not possess complete faith.) «كنّا نخوض مع الخائضین» B: A group that preserves itself within a corrupt society. (Like the Companions of the Cave.) «اِنّهم فتیة آمنوا بربّهم» C: A group that changes and reforms the corrupt society. (Like the Prophets and the Awliyā' [saints].) «یزكّیهم و یعلّمهم الكتاب و الحكمة» It is mentioned in narrations that the Companions of the Cave will be among the companions and helpers of Imam Mahdi (عجّل اللّه تعالى فرجه).(15)

15) منتخب الاثر، ص 485.

Messages

1- Storytelling and narrating true and factual accounts is not the work of just anyone. «نحن نقصّ» 2- The story of the Companions of the Cave is important and beneficial. Nabā' (نبأ) means important and beneficial news. 3- Utilizing historical truths and lessons is one of the educational methods of the Quran. «نحن نقصّ نبأهم بالحقّ» 4- The stories of the Quran are true; they are neither fabricated nor mixed with illusions or distortions. «نقصّ نبأهم بالحقّ» 5- Human movement and effort lead to his growth and guidance. «آمنوابربّهم وزدناهم هدى» 6- Faith and guidance have levels (degrees). «زدناهم هدىً»

Ayah

وَرَبَطْنَا عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ إِذْ قَامُوا۟ فَقَالُوا۟ رَبُّنَا رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ لَن نَّدْعُوَا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ إِلَـٰهًا ۖ لَّقَدْ قُلْنَآ إِذًا شَطَطًا (۱٤)
and fortified their hearts, when they stood up and said, ‘Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. We will never invoke any god besides Him, for then we shall certainly have said an atrocious lie. (14)

Key Points

«شَطَط» (shatat) means excessive deviation from the truth and uttering falsehoods that are in no way acceptable.

Messages

1- The tranquility and steadfastness of hearts are in the hands of Allah. «رَبَطنا»

2- In the face of societal deviations, remaining passive is not permissible; one must rise up. «اذقاموا»

3- Divine assistance is granted under the shadow of faith, unity, rising up, and reliance upon Him. «رَبَطنا على قلوبهم اذ قاموا»

4- Rising up against the oppressor requires firm faith and a confident heart. «ربَطنا على قلوبهم اذ قاموا»

5- True faith is accompanied by rising up. «ربَطنا على قلوبهم اذ قاموا»

6- A true monotheist never resorts to polytheism (shirk). «لن نَدعوا من دونه الهاً»

7- The fruit of Monotheism in Lordship (Tawhid al-Rububiyyah), «ربّنا ربّ السموات و الارض», is Monotheism in servitude (Tawhid al-'Ubūdiyyah). «لن نَدعوا من دونه الهاً»

8- Polytheism (shirk) is a remote and flagrant deviation that is never forgivable. «شَططاً»

Ayah

هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ قَوْمُنَا ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ ءَالِهَةً ۖ لَّوْلَا يَأْتُونَ عَلَيْهِم بِسُلْطَـٰنٍۭ بَيِّنٍ ۖ فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا (۱٥)
These—our people—have taken gods besides Him. Why do they not bring any clear authority touching them? So who is a greater wrongdoer than he who fabricates a lie against Allah? (15)

Key Points

| Although the word iftira' (fabrication) inherently contains the notion of falsehood, the word kidhban (lying) is still mentioned in the Ayah. This may be because iftira' is of two types: sometimes the attribution is possible, but sometimes the attribution is impossible, and polytheism (shirk) is of the second type. This is because a partner for infinite power and knowledge is impossible.

Messages

1- Indifference towards the misguidance and deviation of others is forbidden. «هؤلاء قومنا» (The Companions of the Cave were distressed by the deviation of their people.)

2- The greatest concern of the men of God is the ideological deviation of the people. «اِتّخذوا من دونه آلهة»

3- Imitation and following the environment in matters of belief is not sound logic; one must have clear proof for beliefs. «قومنا اتخّذوا ... لولا یأتون علیهم بسلطان مبین»

4- Polytheism (shirk) is a fabrication against Allah and is without proof. «ممّن افترى على اللّه»

5- Lying is oppression (zulm), and the liar is an oppressor (zalim), and the one who fabricates a lie against Allah is the greatest oppressor. «فمَن اظلم»

Ayah

وَإِذِ ٱعْتَزَلْتُمُوهُمْ وَمَا يَعْبُدُونَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ فَأْوُۥٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلْكَهْفِ يَنشُرْ لَكُمْ رَبُّكُم مِّن رَّحْمَتِهِۦ وَيُهَيِّئْ لَكُم مِّنْ أَمْرِكُم مِّرْفَقًا (۱٦)
When you have dissociated yourselves from them and from what they worship except Allah, then take refuge in the Cave. Your Lord will unfold His mercy for you, and He will help you on to ease in your affair.’ (16)

Key Points

This Ayah is narrated from the leader of the Companions of the Cave, who gives hope of Divine mercy and relief to the young men accompanying him.(16)

16) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The true monotheist abandons both the idols and separates himself from the idolaters. «اعتزلتموهم و ما یعبدون»

2- Worshippers of Allah are exposed to threats from the polytheists. «فأووا الى الكهف»

3- Dwelling in a cave within an atmosphere of monotheism is better than dwelling in a city within an atmosphere of polytheism. «فأووا الى الكهف» Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) also preferred the prison over the palace and said: «ربّ السجن اَحبّ الى»(17)

4- Whoever rises up for Allah and abandons his dwelling and society through migration will be covered by Divine favors. «ینشر لكم»

5- Hardships are temporary and are a prelude to comfort. «یُهیّى ء لكم من أمركم مِرفَقاً»17) Yusuf, 33.

Ayah

۞ وَتَرَى ٱلشَّمْسَ إِذَا طَلَعَت تَّزَٰوَرُ عَن كَهْفِهِمْ ذَاتَ ٱلْيَمِينِ وَإِذَا غَرَبَت تَّقْرِضُهُمْ ذَاتَ ٱلشِّمَالِ وَهُمْ فِى فَجْوَةٍ مِّنْهُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ مَن يَهْدِ ٱللَّهُ فَهُوَ ٱلْمُهْتَدِ ۖ وَمَن يُضْلِلْ فَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُۥ وَلِيًّا مُّرْشِدًا (۱۷)
You may see the sun, when it rises, slanting toward the right of their cave, and, when it sets, cut across them towards the left, and they are in a cavern within it. That is one of Allah’s signs. Whomever Allah guides is rightly guided, and whomever He leads astray, you will never find for him any guardian or guide. (17)

Key Points

The Cave of the Companions of the Cave (Aṣḥāb al-Kahf), geographically, was neither facing east nor west, but was located in the northeast, such that the sunlight never shone into the depth of the cave. There is disagreement regarding the location of the cave; some say it was in the mountains surrounding Damascus, which is famous as the Cave of the Companions of the Cave. Others consider it to be near the city of Amman, the capital of Jordan, where several graves are located, and the image of a dog is on its wall. Above the cave is an ancient monastery, and Muslims have also built a mosque there.(18) In any case, the characteristics of the cave are an example of the Divine Mercy that was promised in the previous Ayah.

18) Tafsir Furqan.

Messages

1- Divine protection is not always in the form of a miracle; sometimes natural factors serve as the means and context for protection, as was the case in the protection of the Companions of the Cave. «ترى الشمس...» (The lack of direct sunlight, the presence of a breeze, and the spaciousness of the cave were natural factors contributing to the well-being of the Companions of the Cave.)

2- The success (Tawfīq) of faith, abandoning the polytheistic people, and taking refuge in a cave with those specific characteristics, cannot happen except through the Divine Will and Guidance. «ذلك من آیات اللّه»

Ayah

وَتَحْسَبُهُمْ أَيْقَاظًا وَهُمْ رُقُودٌ ۚ وَنُقَلِّبُهُمْ ذَاتَ ٱلْيَمِينِ وَذَاتَ ٱلشِّمَالِ ۖ وَكَلْبُهُم بَـٰسِطٌ ذِرَاعَيْهِ بِٱلْوَصِيدِ ۚ لَوِ ٱطَّلَعْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَوَلَّيْتَ مِنْهُمْ فِرَارًا وَلَمُلِئْتَ مِنْهُمْ رُعْبًا (۱۸)
You will suppose them to be awake, although they are asleep. We turn them to the right and to the left, and their dog [lies] stretching its forelegs at the threshold. If you come upon them, you will surely turn to flee from them, and you will surely be filled with a terror of them. (18)

Key Points

According to Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan, the apparent meaning of the phrase "وكَلبُهم باسِط زراعیه" (and their dog stretching out its forelegs) is that the dog lived for 309 years and served as a guard. Yes, if Allah wills, in one cave, He protects His Prophet with a spider's web and makes the disbelievers despair and regret, and in another cave, He preserves believers from the hand of a tyrant through the sitting and guarding of a dog.

Messages

1- One of Allah's blessings is the turning (or shifting) of humans during sleep. «نُقلّبهم»

2- Allah is the Creator of means and the Annihilator of means (He controls cause and effect), and the alteration of factors and effects is among the Divine signs. «تَحسبهم اَیقاظاً و هم رُقود» (Quantitatively, Allah changed the sleep of the Companions of the Cave from a few hours to 309 years, and qualitatively, He changed it to a sleep where their eyes were open and rolling in their sockets.)

3- Sometimes animals are also commissioned for Divine purposes. We should not be heedless of the role of animals in human life. «و كلبهم باسط»

4- The appearance and countenance of individuals are not the criterion for correct recognition. Perhaps an appearance that people flee from belongs to one of Allah's saints.(19) «لوِ اطّلعتَ علیهم لَولّیت منهم فِراراً» (19) Perhaps the fear and terror were due to the change in their appearance, intended for their protection so that no one would dare approach them.

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ بَعَثْنَـٰهُمْ لِيَتَسَآءَلُوا۟ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ قَالَ قَآئِلٌ مِّنْهُمْ كَمْ لَبِثْتُمْ ۖ قَالُوا۟ لَبِثْنَا يَوْمًا أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ ۚ قَالُوا۟ رَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا لَبِثْتُمْ فَٱبْعَثُوٓا۟ أَحَدَكُم بِوَرِقِكُمْ هَـٰذِهِۦٓ إِلَى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ فَلْيَنظُرْ أَيُّهَآ أَزْكَىٰ طَعَامًا فَلْيَأْتِكُم بِرِزْقٍ مِّنْهُ وَلْيَتَلَطَّفْ وَلَا يُشْعِرَنَّ بِكُمْ أَحَدًا (۱۹)
So it was that We aroused them [from sleep] so that they might question one another. One of them said, ‘How long have you stayed [here]?’ They said, ‘We have stayed a day, or part of a day.’ They said, ‘Your Lord knows best how long you have stayed. Send one of you to the city with this money. Let him observe which of them has the purest food, and bring you provisions from there. Let him be attentive, and let him not make anyone aware of you. (19)

Key Points

Wariq (وَرِق) refers to dirhams (silver coins) upon which the image of the king was stamped. The phrase «وَلْیَتلَطّف» (and let him be subtle/gentle) is located in the middle of the Holy Quran. It means gentleness and vigilance combined with kindness. This itself is a grace (lutf) that the middle word of the Quran is composed of grace, subtlety, and kindness. In struggles against corrupt societies, unity among forces is an important factor. In this Ayah, the word kum (كُم - meaning 'your' or 'you all') is mentioned four times:

A: In preparing food, we are all one. «احدكم» (one of you) B: The money is for everyone. «بورقكم» (with your silver coins) C: The food that is prepared is for everyone. «فلیأتكم» (let him bring you) D: Protecting the lives of everyone is necessary. «لایُشعِرنّ بكم» (let no one perceive you)

Messages

1- No task is difficult for Allah, neither putting them to sleep for 309 years nor waking them up in such a manner. «كذلك بَعثناهم»

2- Do not be surprised by the gathering and resurrection on the Day of Judgment; every awakening from sleep is a type of resurrection (ba'th wa nushoor). «بَعثنا»

3- Awakening from sleep is also by Divine Will. «بَعثنا»

4- Waking the Companions of the Cave had two benefits: one for themselves, which was the posing of questions, «لیتسائلوا» (so that they might question one another); and one for others, which was an example of the Resurrection and the raising up (ba'th) of the Day of Judgment. «كذلك بعثناهم»

5- We should not express opinions about what we do not know. «ربّكم اعلم بما لبثتم»

6- Even a small group must have a leader and a responsible person. (Among the Companions of the Cave, there was one person who commanded, forbade, and questioned.) «قال قائل... فابعثوا»

7- Taking action for one's livelihood does not contradict reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul). The Companions of the Cave both possessed money and currency and took action to prepare food. «فابعثوا احدكم بورقكم هذه»

8- Agency (Wikalat) and accepting agency on behalf of the group is permissible. «فابعثوا احدكم»

9- Money and currency have an ancient and historical background. «بوَرقكم»

10- Not every coin can be considered the price of a commodity. «بورقكم هذه»

11- Devout believers do not eat just any food; the type or quantity of food is not the issue for them, rather, being pure (pak) and lawful (halal) is important. «أزكى طعاماً، رزقٍ منه»

12- A believer must be discreet and vigilant, and through gentleness, dissimulation (Taqiyyah), and safety principles, he should not alert the enemy to his presence. «ولیتلطّف». The person who accepts responsibility must exercise the necessary precautions.

Ayah

إِنَّهُمْ إِن يَظْهَرُوا۟ عَلَيْكُمْ يَرْجُمُوكُمْ أَوْ يُعِيدُوكُمْ فِى مِلَّتِهِمْ وَلَن تُفْلِحُوٓا۟ إِذًا أَبَدًا (۲٠)
Indeed should they prevail over you, they will [either] stone you [to death], or force you back into their creed, and then you will never be saved. ’ (20)

Messages

1- In certain situations, dissimulation (Taqiyyah) is obligatory to preserve life and faith. «لایُشعرنّ... یَرجُموكم او یُعیدوكم ...»

2- Whoever neglects the protection of himself and his faith and becomes deviated will never succeed. «لن تفلحوا»

3- The logic of "If you don't want to be disgraced, blend in with the crowd" contradicts the Quran. «یُعیدوكم فى ملّتهم ولن تفلحوا»

4- The oppression of the environment, the coercion of society, and the government often deviate and ruin the general populace, but one must stand against it. «یُعیدوكم فى ملّتهم ولن تفلحوا اذاً ابداً»

5- Being stoned in the path of truth is not a flaw, but deviation and apostasy are flaws. «یرجموكم او یعیدوكم فى ملّتهم و لن تفلحوا اذا ابدا»

6- The apostate will never succeed. «یعیدوكم فى ملّتهم ولن تفلحوا اذاً ابداً»

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَعْثَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ لِيَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَأَنَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهَآ إِذْ يَتَنَـٰزَعُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَمْرَهُمْ ۖ فَقَالُوا۟ ٱبْنُوا۟ عَلَيْهِم بُنْيَـٰنًا ۖ رَّبُّهُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِهِمْ ۚ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ غَلَبُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَمْرِهِمْ لَنَتَّخِذَنَّ عَلَيْهِم مَّسْجِدًا (۲۱)
So it was that We let them come upon them, that they might know that Allah’s promise is true, and that there is no doubt in the Hour. As they disputed among themselves about their matter, they said, ‘Build a building over them. Their Lord knows them best.’ Those who had the say in their matter said, ‘We will set up a place of worship over them.’ (21)

Key Points

The term I'thār refers to gaining knowledge accidentally and without searching, just as the people of that time, without effort, and through the Companions' money and coin, became aware of their important secret. The story of the Companions of the Cave raises the issues of divine power and will, courage, detachment from the world, migration (Hijra), dissimulation (Taqiyya), divine aid, and lawful sustenance.

Messages

  1. Guidance and instruction that is experiential, tangible, and demonstrative is among the best forms of guidance. «اَعثَرنا علیهم لیعلموا»
  2. None of Allah's actions are futile or vain. «اَعثَرنا...لیعلموا أنّ وعد اللّه حقّ»
  3. Beliefs must be based on knowledge. «لیعلموا أنّ وعد اللّه حقّ»
  4. The power that can keep a human alive without food for more than three centuries can also resurrect the dead. «أنّ وعداللّه حقّ»
  5. Humans easily pass by some historical events and are heedless of their educational impact. The Companions of the Cave passed away after the incident was discovered, and the people were thinking of commemoration and building a tomb, not of taking a lesson or learning. «ابنُوا علیهم بنیاناً»
  6. Most superficial and simple-minded people are not willing to work themselves and tell others to do the work, «قالوا ابنوا» (They said, "Build"), but strong individuals say, "We will do it ourselves." «لنتّخذنّ»
  7. Building a mosque over the graves of Allah's saints has a Quranic root. «لنتّخذنّ علیهم مسجداً»
  8. Sacred monuments should be in sacred places and on sacred occasions. A mosque beside the cave of the migrating monotheists has greater value. «لنتّخذنّ علیهم مسجداً»
  9. We should use every opportunity to promote religion. «لنتّخذنّ علیهم مسجداً» (If a building is to be constructed as a memorial, let us build a mosque, which has many spiritual and material benefits.)

Ayah

سَيَقُولُونَ ثَلَـٰثَةٌ رَّابِعُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ وَيَقُولُونَ خَمْسَةٌ سَادِسُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ رَجْمًۢا بِٱلْغَيْبِ ۖ وَيَقُولُونَ سَبْعَةٌ وَثَامِنُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ ۚ قُل رَّبِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ بِعِدَّتِهِم مَّا يَعْلَمُهُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ ۗ فَلَا تُمَارِ فِيهِمْ إِلَّا مِرَآءً ظَـٰهِرًا وَلَا تَسْتَفْتِ فِيهِم مِّنْهُمْ أَحَدًا (۲۲)
They will say, ‘[They are] three; their dog is the fourth of them,’ and say, ‘[They are] five, their dog is the sixth of them,’ taking a shot at the invisible. They will say, ‘[They are] seven, their dog is the eighth of them.’ Say, ‘My Lord knows best their number, and none knows them except a few.’ So do not dispute concerning them, except for a seeming dispute, and do not question about them any of them. (22)

Key Points

Regarding rabi'uhum (the fourth of them) and sadisuhum (the sixth of them), the word waw (and) is not used, but accompanying thaminuhum (the eighth of them), the waw is present. Perhaps this is because the theory that they were three or five people comes from the ignorant, which Allah refers to as rajman bil-ghayb (a conjecture into the unseen). However, the theory that they were seven people comes from the believers and those who are precise (20). For this reason, the phrase rajman bil-ghayb is not used, and out of respect for them, the Companions of the Cave and their dog are separated by the waw of conjunction. If movements are valuable and people have a goal, then their dependents, associates, and even accompanying animals must be taken into account. Being an animal or being ritually impure (najis) is not a reason for worthlessness. The dog is mentioned three times in this verse, and in verse 18, the positive role of the Companions of the Cave's dog is also mentioned: «وكلبهم باسط ذراعیه بالوصید» (And their dog stretching forth its forelegs at the entrance). Guesswork is criticized in various verses of this Surah: «ربّكم اعلم بمالبثتم» Ayah 19, «ربّهم اعلم بهم» Ayah 21, «ربّى اعلم بعدّتهم» Ayah 22, and «قل اللّه اعلم بما لَبثوا» Ayah 26.

(20) This theory was narrated by Ibn Abbas from Amir al-Mu'minin عليه السلام.

Messages

1- The Quran gives news of the future. «سیقولون» (They will say)

2- Do not speak without evidence and do not rely on estimation or conjecture. «رجماً بالغیب»

3- In unnecessary matters, one must refrain from debate and argument. «لا تمار فیهم ...»

4- Instead of thinking about the number, think about the goal, and do not pursue useless knowledge. «لا تستفت فیهم منهم احداً»

5- Knowing and asking everyone is not permissible; do not ask the unqualified. «لاتستفت... منهم»

Ayahs

وَلَا تَقُولَنَّ لِشَا۟ىْءٍ إِنِّى فَاعِلٌ ذَٰلِكَ غَدًا (۲۳)
Do not say about anything, ‘I will indeed do it tomorrow,’ (23)
إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَٱذْكُر رَّبَّكَ إِذَا نَسِيتَ وَقُلْ عَسَىٰٓ أَن يَهْدِيَنِ رَبِّى لِأَقْرَبَ مِنْ هَـٰذَا رَشَدًا (۲٤)
without [adding], ‘God willing.’ And when you forget, remember your Lord, and say, ‘Maybe my Lord will guide me to [something] more akin to rectitude than this.’ (24)

Key Points

Saying "in shaa Allah" (if Allah wills), which conveys belief in Divine power and will, is the characteristic phrase of the friends of Allah (Awliya Allah). As it is also narrated in the Qur'an from the tongues of the Prophets; Jacob says to his children: «اُدخلو مصر اِن شاءاللّه آمنین» (21) Enter Egypt, if Allah wills, you will be safe. Khidr says to Moses: «ستجدنى اِن شاءاللّه صابراً» (22) If Allah wills, you will find me patient. Prophet Shu'ayb says to Prophet Moses: «ستجدنى اِن شاءاللّه من الصالحین» (23) And Prophet Ishmael says to his father Prophet Abraham: «ستجدنى اِن شاءاللّه من الصابرین» (24) Sacrifice me, for by the will of Allah you will find me among the patient. Of course, the purpose of saying "in shaa Allah" and "a'udhu billah" (I seek refuge in Allah) and the like is not mere lip service, but rather possessing such insight in all aspects of life, within one's belief and heart. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Do not forget "in shaa Allah" even in your writings. One day, He ordered a letter to be written, and when He saw the letter without "in shaa Allah," He said: «كیف رجوتم أن یتمّ هذا؟» How do you hope that this matter will be completed? (25) The Prophet of Islam, upon entering the graveyard, would say: «وانّا اِن شاءاللّه بكم لاحقون» If Allah wills, we too shall join you, even though death is certain. (26) While man possesses will and is free, he is not independent, nor have all affairs been entrusted to him such that he can accomplish anything without the will of Allah. That is, man is neither subject to determinism (Jabr) nor has complete authority (Ikhtiyar) over all matters; rather, human freedom exists under the shadow of Allah's will. «الاّ أن یشاء اللّه»

21) Yusuf, 99. 22) Kahf, 69. 23) Qasas, 27. 24) Saffat, 102. 25) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 26) Tafsir Kashf al-Asrar.

Messages

1- Let us not forget Allah when speaking and making decisions. «ولا تقولنّ...»

2- Never consider ourselves independent of Allah, and never speak definitively about performing an action, no matter how small, in the future. «لاتقولنّ... اِنّى فاعل...»

3- Do not rely on our own resources and abilities, as the availability of prerequisites does not guarantee the definite completion of the task. «لاتقولنّ... اِنّى فاعل...» Even concerning what we are certain of, we must say "in shaa Allah."

4- Man is constantly in need of Allah and must consider himself dependent on the Divine Will in every matter. «لا تقولنّ لشى ء اِنّى فاعل...»

5- Compensating for what has been missed is necessary. «واذكر ربّك اذا نَسیت»

6- Even the Prophets needed Divine instruction and guidance. «قل عسى أن یهدین ربّى»

7- Expressing hope for Divine assistance is one of the correct methods of supplication (Du'a). «عسى...»

8- Guidance has stages, and all of them are in the hands of Allah. «أن یهدین ربّى لاقرب من...»

9- One must find the shortcut and the nearest path to truth and righteousness. «اقرب من هذا...»

10- Attaining superior growth (Rushd) is the aspiration of the Prophets. «عسى أن یهدین ربّى لاقرب...»

11- Growth and reaching higher stages exist even for the Prophets. «عسى أن یهدین ربّى لاقرب من هذا رشدا»

12- The continuation of the remembrance of Allah (Dhikr) is the shortest path to attaining growth. «واذكر ربّك... عسى أن یهدین ربّى لاقرب من هذا رشدا»

Ayahs

وَلَبِثُوا۟ فِى كَهْفِهِمْ ثَلَـٰثَ مِا۟ئَةٍ سِنِينَ وَٱزْدَادُوا۟ تِسْعًا (۲٥)
They remained in the Cave for three hundred years, and added nine more [to that number]. (25)
قُلِ ٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا لَبِثُوا۟ ۖ لَهُۥ غَيْبُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ أَبْصِرْ بِهِۦ وَأَسْمِعْ ۚ مَا لَهُم مِّن دُونِهِۦ مِن وَلِىٍّ وَلَا يُشْرِكُ فِى حُكْمِهِۦٓ أَحَدًا (۲٦)
Say, ‘Allah knows best how long they remained. To Him belongs the Unseen of the heavens and the earth. How well does He see! How well does He hear! They have no guardian besides Him, and none shares with Him in His judgement.’ (26)

Key Points

The Jews asked Imam Ali عليه السلام about the duration of the stay of the Companions of the Cave in the cave. He replied: 309 years. They said: In our book, it is written as 300 years! He replied: The 9 years are due to the difference between the solar and lunar years. (27) In Tafsir Maraghi, this nine-year difference is considered a sign of the miraculous nature of the Quran, showing how precise it is and that it took into account the difference between lunar and solar years. It might be said that the duration was exactly three hundred years, and people added nine years to it, "وازدادوا تسعاً" (and they added nine). Therefore, Allah replies: "قل اللّه اعلم بما لبثوا" (Say: Allah knows best how long they stayed).

Human lifespan does not have a fixed limit; it is not like water that boils at one hundred degrees and freezes at zero degrees. In experiments, the lifespan of some living creatures has been increased up to twelve times. As reported in the newspapers: A frozen fish, dating back thousands of years, found among the polar ice, resumed life after being placed in lukewarm water. (28)

If statistics are presented simply and ordinarily, they might be forgotten or the listener might not pay special attention to them. For example, if a patient tells a doctor: "I have been waiting for four hours," or says, "four hours minus 7 minutes," the doctor's attention differs. This is because figures are sometimes used to express general quantity and are not very precise, but if they are stated minutely and accurately, they attract the listener. Therefore, regarding the duration of Prophet Nuh's (Noah's) preaching, He says: "a thousand years save fifty years," to announce that the figures are precise. In this Ayah too, He says: three hundred years, and then says: nine years added, to show that the statistics are accurate.

27) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan. 28) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- The method of stating statistics and numbers must be precise. "ثلاث مأئة سنین وازدادوا تسعاً"

2- Precision in stating figures and statistics makes them more lasting in the mind. "ثلاث مأئة سنین وازدادوا تسعاً"

Ayah

وَٱتْلُ مَآ أُوحِىَ إِلَيْكَ مِن كِتَابِ رَبِّكَ ۖ لَا مُبَدِّلَ لِكَلِمَـٰتِهِۦ وَلَن تَجِدَ مِن دُونِهِۦ مُلْتَحَدًا (۲۷)
Recite what has been revealed to you from the Book of your Lord. Nothing can change His words, and you will never find any refuge besides Him. (27)

Key Points

One of the pretexts of the opponents of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was that they requested Him to change and alter the Quran, saying: «ائت بقرآنٍ غیر هذا او بَدّله»(29) Bring another Quran or change this one. The Prophet replied to them, saying: This is not in my power; changing the verses is solely the work of Allah.

29) Yunus, 15.

Messages

1- Revelation (Wahy) and the descent of heavenly books are necessary requirements of Divine Lordship and are in the path of human nurturing. «ما اوحى الیك من كتاب ربّك»

2- The Quran has been protected from any kind of change or distortion. Just as the Prophet of Islam is the Seal of the Prophets, the Quran is also the final heavenly book. «لامبدّل لكلماته»

Ayah

وَٱصْبِرْ نَفْسَكَ مَعَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُم بِٱلْغَدَوٰةِ وَٱلْعَشِىِّ يُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَهُۥ ۖ وَلَا تَعْدُ عَيْنَاكَ عَنْهُمْ تُرِيدُ زِينَةَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَا تُطِعْ مَنْ أَغْفَلْنَا قَلْبَهُۥ عَن ذِكْرِنَا وَٱتَّبَعَ هَوَىٰهُ وَكَانَ أَمْرُهُۥ فُرُطًا (۲۸)
Content yourself with the company of those who supplicate their Lord morning and evening, desiring His Face, and do not lose sight of them, desiring the glitter of the life of this world. And do not obey him whose heart We have made oblivious to Our remembrance, and who follows his own desires, and whose conduct is [mere] profligacy. (28)

Key Points

Throughout history, the wealthy and affluent disbelievers consistently made the condition for their belief the expulsion of the poor and destitute. For instance, they made such a request to Noah, and the Prophet replied to them: «ما أنا بِطارد الّذین آمنوا»(30) (I am not one to drive away those who have believed). (I do not expel the believers because of their poverty.) During the time of the Prophet of Islam, they also demanded that he drive away the poor, such as Salman, Abu Dharr, Ammar, and Khabbab, so that they (the wealthy) would believe and join him. This Ayah condemns such a thought and action.

Imam Sadiq and Imam Baqir عليهما السلام said: The meaning of calling upon Allah in the morning and evening is the establishment of prayer (Salat).(31)

30) Hud, 29. 31) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The wealthy tried to dissuade the Prophet from associating with the poor believers, and the Prophet was commanded to resist these efforts. «واصبر نفسك»

2- Sympathizing with the poor is difficult, but it must be endured. «واصبر نفسك»

3- Adherence to supplication (Du'a) and prayer is a condition for individuals' worthiness of companionship and association. «واصبر نفسك مع الّذین یدعون ربّهم...»

4- A leader must be sympathetic and companionable toward the deprived, not indifferent.(32) «واصبر نفسك مع الذّین... ولا تَعدُ عیناك عنهم»

5- Constant and sincere supplication is valuable. «بالغَداوَةوالعشىّ یریدون وجهه»

6- One must remember Allah at the beginning and end of every day. «بالغداوَة والعشى»

7- We should not distance ourselves from the poor in order to gain the world and the satisfaction of the capitalists. «تُرید زینة الحیاة الدنیا»

8- The ugliest thing is for the people to turn their attention to Allah, while the leader turns his attention to the world. «یریدون وجهه... و لا تعد عیناك عنهم تُرید زینة الحیاة الدنیا»

9- The danger of worldliness is so great that Allah warns even the Prophets about it. «ولاتعد عیناك عنهم تُرید زینة الحیاة الدنیا»

10- Whoever pursues the world deviates from the path of the divine travelers. «یریدون وجهه... ترید زینة الحیاة الدنیا» Worldliness is the opposite of God-centeredness.

11- The punishment for worldliness is heedlessness of the remembrance of Allah. «ترید زینة الحیاة الدنیا ولا تطع من اغفلنا قلبه»

12- The value of remembering Allah lies in its being rooted in the heart and soul. «اغفلنا قلبه عن ذكرنا»

13- Man falls step by step: first heedlessness, then following desires, and then the path of deviation. «اغفلنا، اتّبع هواه، كان امره فُرطا»

14- Leadership by individuals who are heedless, follow desires, and are excessive (extremist) is forbidden. «اغفلنا، اتبع هواه، كان امره فرطاً»

15- Moderation is a value, and excessiveness is an anti-value. «وكان امره فرطا»

32) In Ayah 52 of Surah Al-An'am, it states: «فتطردهم فتكون من الظّالمین» (and drive them away, so you would be among the wrongdoers). Expelling believers is equivalent to oppression (Zulm).

Ayah

وَقُلِ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ فَمَن شَآءَ فَلْيُؤْمِن وَمَن شَآءَ فَلْيَكْفُرْ ۚ إِنَّآ أَعْتَدْنَا لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ نَارًا أَحَاطَ بِهِمْ سُرَادِقُهَا ۚ وَإِن يَسْتَغِيثُوا۟ يُغَاثُوا۟ بِمَآءٍ كَٱلْمُهْلِ يَشْوِى ٱلْوُجُوهَ ۚ بِئْسَ ٱلشَّرَابُ وَسَآءَتْ مُرْتَفَقًا (۲۹)
And say, ‘[This is] the truth from your Lord: let anyone who wishes believe it, and let anyone who wishes disbelieve it.’ Indeed, We have prepared for the wrongdoers a Fire whose curtains will surround them [on all sides]. If they cry out for help, they will be helped with a water like molten copper, which will scald their faces. What an evil drink, and how ill a resting place! (29)

Key Points

The Ayah seems to be a response to those who set conditions for their belief, saying: The condition for our conversion is that you expel the poor. Although people are free to choose faith or disbelief, they must know that the ultimate outcome of disbelief is the burning torment of the Day of Judgment. Surādiq (سُرادِق) means a pavilion or tent, and it has appeared only once in the Quran, specifically concerning the inhabitants of Hell. Imam Reza عليه السلام said to one of the employees of the Tāghūt (tyrant): If I were to fall from a mountain and be torn to pieces, it would be better than being an agent of the Tāghūt, unless I were solving a problem for the believers. Then He said: Allah will place the agents of the Tāghūt in a pavilion of fire until He is finished with the reckoning of creation.

Messages

1- The only source of truth is Allah. «الحقّ من ربّكم»

2- The request to expel the believing poor is not the truth; rather, the truth is that which comes from Allah. «قل الحقّ من ربّكم»

3- The inclination or lack of inclination of people does not affect the truthfulness of the religion. «قل الحقّ من ربّكم فمَن شاء فلیؤمن و مَن شاء فلیَكفر»

4- The Prophet is responsible for conveying the message, and humans are free in accepting faith or disbelief. «فمَن شاء فلیؤمن و مَن شاء فلیفكر»

The merchandise of disbelief and faith is not without customers; some prefer that, and some prefer this.

5- Disbelief is oppression against oneself. «فلیكفر، اَعتدنا للظالمین»

6- Hell is prepared from now. «اَعتدنا»

7- Man's destiny depends on his inclinations and deeds. «اَعتدنا للظالمین ناراً احاط بها سُرادقها» The desires and corruptions that encompass a person will, in the Hereafter, be a fire that surrounds him.

8- Attention to the grim fate of the disbelievers plays an important role in human development. «للظالمین ناراً احاط بها...»

9- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical. «یَشوِى الوجوه»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ إِنَّا لَا نُضِيعُ أَجْرَ مَنْ أَحْسَنَ عَمَلًا (۳٠)
As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds—indeed We do not waste the reward of those who are good in deeds. (30)

Key Points

Allah does not waste our righteous deeds; rather, it is our bad traits and deeds that cause the nullification and destruction of good works. Every righteous deed can be performed in several ways: in an ordinary manner, a good manner, and an excellent manner. This Ayah encourages the most excellent way. Indeed, sometimes the ingredients are good, but the method of cooking them is only good or average.

Messages

1- In upbringing (tarbiyah), threat and glad tidings, and fear and hope, are necessary alongside each other. (The previous Ayah was a threat, and this Ayah is glad tidings.) «اِنّا اَعتدنا للظالمین... انّا لا نُضیع أجر...»

2- Divine thought and motivation precede righteous action. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات»

3- In the Islamic worldview, nothing remains hidden from the Divine reckoning, nor is the reward of any deed wasted. «اِنّا لانضیع...»

4- The criterion for the value of a deed is quality, not quantity. «أحسن عملاً» not «اكثر عملاً»

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتُ عَدْنٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهِمُ ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ يُحَلَّوْنَ فِيهَا مِنْ أَسَاوِرَ مِن ذَهَبٍ وَيَلْبَسُونَ ثِيَابًا خُضْرًا مِّن سُندُسٍ وَإِسْتَبْرَقٍ مُّتَّكِـِٔينَ فِيهَا عَلَى ٱلْأَرَآئِكِ ۚ نِعْمَ ٱلثَّوَابُ وَحَسُنَتْ مُرْتَفَقًا (۳۱)
For such there will be the gardens of Eden with streams running in them. They will be adorned therein with bracelets of gold and wear green garments of silk and brocade, reclining therein on couches. How excellent a reward, and how good a resting place! (31)

Key Points

'Adn' means residence and dwelling place. 'Asāwir' is the plural of 'Aswirah,' meaning bracelet. 'Sundus' refers to thin silk, and 'Istabraq' refers to thick silk (brocade). 'Arā'ik' is the plural of 'Arīkah,' meaning a royal couch or throne covered with a canopy (or netting). In the two preceding verses, we read concerning the inhabitants of Hell: «بِئسَ الشّراب و ساءت مُرتفقا» (What an evil drink and what a bad resting place). And this verse says concerning the inhabitants of Paradise: «نعم الثواب و حَسُنت مُرتفقا» (What an excellent reward and what a beautiful resting place). Considering that every type of clothing, material, and color is available to the inhabitants of Paradise, the specific mention of green clothing might refer to their formal or general attire, which they use to adorn themselves while reclining on the couches.

Messages

1- The rivers of Paradise flow both beneath the trees, «تحتها الانهار» (33), and beneath the palaces of Paradise. «من تحتهم الانهار»

2- The enjoyment of jewelry by the inhabitants of Paradise shows that humans naturally love adornment and beauty. «یُحلّون فیها من أساور»

3- Faith, acting upon divine commands, and abstaining from unlawful luxuries in this world are the keys to attaining eternal luxuries in the Hereafter. «یُحلّون فیها من أساور...»

4- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is both spiritual and physical, because if it were only spiritual, there would be no need for gold, silk, and couches. «یُحلّون فیها من أساور...»

5- The color green is the color of Paradise. «ثیاباً خُضراً» (33) Al-Baqarah, 25.

Ayahs

۞ وَٱضْرِبْ لَهُم مَّثَلًا رَّجُلَيْنِ جَعَلْنَا لِأَحَدِهِمَا جَنَّتَيْنِ مِنْ أَعْنَـٰبٍ وَحَفَفْنَـٰهُمَا بِنَخْلٍ وَجَعَلْنَا بَيْنَهُمَا زَرْعًا (۳۲)
Draw for them the parable of two men for each of whom We had made two gardens of vines, and We had surrounded them with date palms, and placed crops between them. (32)
كِلْتَا ٱلْجَنَّتَيْنِ ءَاتَتْ أُكُلَهَا وَلَمْ تَظْلِم مِّنْهُ شَيْـًٔا ۚ وَفَجَّرْنَا خِلَـٰلَهُمَا نَهَرًا (۳۳)
Both gardens yielded their produce without stinting anything of it. And We had set a stream gushing through them. (33)

Key Points

Ukl (أُكُل) means the produce and fruit that is consumable and edible. From these verses, one can derive the principles of gardening or establishing exemplary and captivating orchards, which are: A: Short trees (vines) should be in the middle (a'nāb) and tall trees should be around the orchard (nakhil). B: The orchards should be separated from each other and their surroundings should be open. «جعلنا بینهما» C: The lands in the space between the orchards should be cultivated. «بینهما زرعاً» D: Running water should flow between the orchards. «فجّرنا خلالهما» E: The orchards should not be weak, fragile, or prone to pests. «لم تظلم منه شیئا» Therefore, the best scenery is created by diverse grape orchards (a'nāb), grapes and dates side-by-side (a'nāb wa nakhl), various types of cultivation (zar'an), cultivation surrounded by trees (ḥafafnāhumā), and water flowing beneath the trees and beside the farm (fajjarnā khilālahumā naharā).

These verses seem to be a response to the demand of the wealthy disbelievers who wanted the Prophet to drive the poor away from himself so that they (the wealthy) would gather around him and believe. Allah, in response to them, states: neither is poverty a sign of humiliation nor is wealth a sign of human honor. Just as He told His Prophet in verse 28: «واصبر نفسك مع الّذین یَدعون ربّهم...»

Messages

1- One of the simplest, most innate, and most common methods of conveying a point is the use of analogy and parable. «واضرب لهم مثلاً»

2- The difference between human poverty and wealth is from the Wise God (Allah). «جعلنا لاحدهما...»

3- The true cultivator is Allah, not man. «حَفَفنا، فَجّرنا، جعلنا»

Ayah

وَكَانَ لَهُۥ ثَمَرٌ فَقَالَ لِصَـٰحِبِهِۦ وَهُوَ يُحَاوِرُهُۥٓ أَنَا۠ أَكْثَرُ مِنكَ مَالًا وَأَعَزُّ نَفَرًا (۳٤)
He had abundant fruits, so he said to his companion, as he conversed with him: ‘I have more wealth than you, and am stronger with respect to numbers.’ (34)

Key Points

"Thamar" (ثَمر) is used for various types of fruit, various types of wealth and possessions, and even for the tree itself. Just as we read in Ayah 42: «اُحیط بثمره» (His produce was encompassed [by ruin]), where Allah's wrath encompassed the entire garden and the trees, not just its fruit. "Sahib" (صاحب) means companion and interlocutor, whether the person is a believer, like in this Ayah, or an unbeliever, like in Ayah 37 of this Surah where He says: «قال لصاحبه و هو یُحاوره أكفرت» (He said to his companion while he was conversing with him, "Have you disbelieved...").

Messages

1- Attachment and pride in wealth and tribe are the grounds for transgression. «انَا اكثر منك» (I am greater than you [in wealth and children]).

2- Neither is poverty a sign of humiliation, nor is wealth proof of honor; therefore, we should not boast over the poor. «انَا اكثر منك مالا واعزّ...» (I am greater than you in wealth and mightier...).

Do not look upon the humble ones of the world with contempt, If you see a handle-less jug, lift it with both hands.

Ayah

وَدَخَلَ جَنَّتَهُۥ وَهُوَ ظَالِمٌ لِّنَفْسِهِۦ قَالَ مَآ أَظُنُّ أَن تَبِيدَ هَـٰذِهِۦٓ أَبَدًا (۳٥)
He entered his garden while he wronged himself. He said, ‘I do not think that this will ever perish, (35)

Messages

1- All possessions of man are from Allah, and He is the true Owner. In Ayah 32, He said: «جعلنا لاحدهما جنّتین» (We made for one of them two gardens), and in this Ayah, He says: «دخل جنّته» (He entered his garden).

2- Abundant wealth and blessings are a cause for arrogance. «دخل جنّته... قال» (He entered his garden... he said).

3- Negligence of Allah, self-superiority, belittling others, and being deceived by the material manifestations of the world is oppression against oneself. «ظالم لنفسه» (oppressor to himself).

4- The deviated and heedless person is worse than inanimate objects and plants. Regarding the garden, Allah said in Ayah 23: «ولم تظلم منه شیئاً» (and did not fail [to produce] anything from it), meaning it did not fall short of anything, but regarding the owner of the garden, He said: «ظالم لنفسه».

5- Never assume that blessings and enjoyments are permanent and eternal. «ما اظنّ ان تبید» (I do not think that it will perish).

Ayah

وَمَآ أَظُنُّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ قَآئِمَةً وَلَئِن رُّدِدتُّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّى لَأَجِدَنَّ خَيْرًا مِّنْهَا مُنقَلَبًا (۳٦)
and I do not think that the Hour will ever set in. And even if I am returned to my Lord I will surely find a resort better than this.’ (36)

Key Points

The disbelievers imagine that granting blessings is obligatory upon Allah, and that it is the right of man to always be provided for. As we read in Ayah 50 of Surah Fussilat: «لئن رُجِعتُ الى ربّى اِنّ لى عنده للحُسنى » (If I am returned to my Lord, surely I will have the best [rewards] with Him). [They believe that] even if there is a Resurrection, the best things will be for us. But this way of thinking is rejected in the Quran. Man benefits from the grace and favor of Allah, but if Allah wills, He makes the waters bitter, dries up the trees, destroys the disbelievers, or cuts off revelation to the prophets, lest they imagine that they deserve these blessings without divine grace.

Messages

1- Attachment to the world and worldliness is the basis for denying the Resurrection. «انا اكثر منك... وما اَظنّ الساعة قائمة...»

2- Deniers of the Resurrection have no proof for its negation. «ما اظنّ»

3- Undue and baseless hope and aspiration are condemned. «ولئن رُدِدت»

4- Man naturally inclines toward Allah and spirituality. «الى ربّى»

5- Some people mistakenly consider blessings a sign of their own dignity and worth, and they deem them eternal. «لئن رُدِدتُ الى ربّى لاجدنّ خیراً»

Ayahs

قَالَ لَهُۥ صَاحِبُهُۥ وَهُوَ يُحَاوِرُهُۥٓ أَكَفَرْتَ بِٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكَ مِن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ ثُمَّ سَوَّىٰكَ رَجُلًا (۳۷)
His companion said to him, as he conversed with him: ‘Do you disbelieve in Him who created you from dust, then from a drop of [seminal] fluid, then fashioned you as a man? (37)
لَّـٰكِنَّا۠ هُوَ ٱللَّهُ رَبِّى وَلَآ أُشْرِكُ بِرَبِّىٓ أَحَدًا (۳۸)
But I [say], ‘‘He is Allah, my Lord,’’ and I do not ascribe any partner to my Lord. (38)

Key Points

| "Muḥāwarah" (محاوره) means conversation, the exchange of words, and responding to one another. The word "lakinnā" (لكنّا) was originally "lakin anā" (لكن أنا).

Messages

1- Dialogue and free discussion among different schools of thought are accepted by the Quran. «و هو یُحاوره»

2- When facing the wealthy and powerful, engage in guidance with complete dignity and without feeling inferior. «قال له صاحبه... أكفرت»

3- Sometimes, attachment to wealth and riches is the basis for a person's disbelief in Allah and the Day of Judgment. «انَا اكثر منك... أكفرت بالّذى خلقك»

4- The cure for one who boasts about his wealth and relatives is reminding him of his earthly origin. «انَا اكثر منك ... خلقك من تُرابٍ ثمّ من نُطفة»

5- Denying the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is denying Allah. He denied the Resurrection by saying «ما اظنّ السّاعة قائمة» (I do not think the Hour will ever occur), but his companion said to him: «أكفرتَ بالّذى خلقك» (Have you disbelieved in Him Who created you?!).

6- Disbelief in Allah warrants astonishment, surprise, and reproach. «اكفرتَ»

7- Objecting to the disbelievers is permissible. «أكفرتَ» (Have you disbelieved?), but it must be accompanied by reasoning and showing the way forward. «الّذى خلقك من تُراب»

8- In educating others and deterring them from deviation, we must speak with reasoning. «أكفرت بالّذى خلقك»

9- In the face of others' doubt and skepticism, the believer must clearly declare his position. «لكنّا هو اللّه» (But as for me, He is Allah).

10- Relying on anything other than Allah is polytheism (Shirk). «أنا اكثر منك... و لكنّا... لااُشرِك بربّى احدا»

11- Pure Monotheism (Tawhid) is that which is accompanied by the negation of all forms of polytheism. «اللّه ربّى و لااُشرِك بربّى احدا»

Ayah

وَلَوْلَآ إِذْ دَخَلْتَ جَنَّتَكَ قُلْتَ مَا شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِٱللَّهِ ۚ إِن تَرَنِ أَنَا۠ أَقَلَّ مِنكَ مَالًا وَوَلَدًا (۳۹)
Why did you not say, when you entered your garden, ‘‘[This is] as Allah has willed! There is no power except by Allah!’’ If you see that I have lesser wealth than you and children, (39)

Key Points

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Every blessing that Allah bestows upon His servant, whether wealth or children, if he says: «ما شاء اللّه ولاقوّة الاّ باللّه» (What Allah wills, and there is no power except with Allah), Allah repels afflictions and distresses from him until He reaches his desire. Then He recited this Ayah. (34) Also, it is mentioned in narrations: The phrase «لاحول ولا قوّة الاّ باللّه» (There is no might and no power except with Allah) causes the repulsion of calamity and the permanence of blessing, and it is a treasure from the treasures of Paradise. (35)

34) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur. 35) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq.

Messages

1- Reprimand and censure must be accompanied by guidance and instruction. «لولا... قلت ماشاءاللّه»

2- Belief alone is not sufficient; one must also affirm it with the tongue. «قلت ماشاءاللّه»

3- When encountering beautiful natural scenes, we should remember Allah, for all blessings are from Him. «لولا اذ دَخلتَ جنّتك قلت ماشاءاللّه»

4- To prevent heedlessness and arrogance, we must not forget the Divine Will when encountering blessings. «ماشاء اللّه»

5- A believer does not lose heart due to a lack of wealth or children. «اِن تَرَنِ» means you see me as lacking, not that I am lacking.

Ayahs

فَعَسَىٰ رَبِّىٓ أَن يُؤْتِيَنِ خَيْرًا مِّن جَنَّتِكَ وَيُرْسِلَ عَلَيْهَا حُسْبَانًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَتُصْبِحَ صَعِيدًا زَلَقًا (٤٠)
maybe my Lord will give me [something] better than your garden, and He will unleash upon it bolts from the sky, so that it becomes a bare plain. (40)
أَوْ يُصْبِحَ مَآؤُهَا غَوْرًا فَلَن تَسْتَطِيعَ لَهُۥ طَلَبًا (٤۱)
Or its water will sink down, so that you will never be able to obtain it.’ (41)

Key Points

The term «زَلق» refers to smooth, plantless ground, so much so that a person's foot slips on it.

Messages

1- Allah’s acts of giving and taking are based on His Lordship and nurturing. «فعسى ربّى أن یُؤتین»

2- The poor should not despair, «انَا اَقلّ... عسى ربّى», because despair and hopelessness are worse than poverty.

3- If Allah wills, the poor person becomes rich, indeed, superior to the rich person. «خیراً من جنّتك»

4- Wishing for the removal of blessings from boastful disbelievers, and invoking curses upon them, is acceptable. «یرسل علیها حُسباناً...»

5- Allah’s wrath is calculated and just. «حُسباناً»

6- The wealthy should not be arrogant about their possessions; perhaps all their wealth will be destroyed in a single moment. «فتُصبِح صَعیداً زَلَقا»

7- Polytheism, disbelief, and boasting transform wealth and greenery into a desert. «فتُصبح صعیداً زَلَقا»

8- Allah’s hand is open for wrath and punishment, whether it comes from the sky or from the earth. «یرسل علیها حُسباناً من السماء - یُصبِح ماؤها غَوراً»

9- In the face of Divine anger and wrath, there is no power or means of escape. «فلن تَستطیع له طَلَباً»

Ayah

وَأُحِيطَ بِثَمَرِهِۦ فَأَصْبَحَ يُقَلِّبُ كَفَّيْهِ عَلَىٰ مَآ أَنفَقَ فِيهَا وَهِىَ خَاوِيَةٌ عَلَىٰ عُرُوشِهَا وَيَقُولُ يَـٰلَيْتَنِى لَمْ أُشْرِكْ بِرَبِّىٓ أَحَدًا (٤۲)
And ruin closed in on his produce, and he began to wring his hands for what he had spent on it, as it lay fallen on its trellises. He was saying, ‘I wish I had not ascribed any partner to my Lord.’ (42)

Key Points

Some, due to arrogance and self-superiority, and by saying, "انا اكثر منك مالاً..." (I am greater than you in wealth...), become heedless of Allah, exit the orbit of Monotheism (Tawhid), and become polytheists (Mushrik). But sooner or later, they will acknowledge the One Lord, whether they are believers (who say) "لااُشرك بربّى احداً" (I will not associate anyone with my Lord) (36) or disbelievers (who will say), "یالیتنى لم اُشرك بربّى احداً" (Oh, I wish I had not associated anyone with my Lord).

36) Kahf, 38.

Messages

1- Allah is encompassing, "واللّه محیط" (37); His grace encompasses everything, "رحمتى وسعت كلّ شى ء" (38), and His wrath is also comprehensive. "اُحیط بثمره"

2- Allah's punishment lies in wait for the arrogant. "اُحیط بثمره"

3- Divine events and punishments are the consequence of our bad thoughts and deeds. Following the disbelief (kufr) and ingratitude of the owner of the garden in the previous verses, "اُحیط بثمره" (His produce was encompassed) is mentioned.

4- Despair and internal states affect a person's appearance and actions. "یُقلّب كفّیه" (He turned his hands over).

5- Worldly seekers first analyze events economically, and then spiritually. (In this verse, regret is first expressed over the money spent, and then sorrow over polytheism/shirk).

6- Worldly punishments are a wake-up call for dormant consciences and heedless hearts. "احیط بثمره ... یقول یالیتنى" 37) Baqarah, 19.

38) A'raf, 156.

Ayahs

وَلَمْ تَكُن لَّهُۥ فِئَةٌ يَنصُرُونَهُۥ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَمَا كَانَ مُنتَصِرًا (٤۳)
He had no party to help him, besides Allah, nor could he help himself. (43)
هُنَالِكَ ٱلْوَلَـٰيَةُ لِلَّهِ ٱلْحَقِّ ۚ هُوَ خَيْرٌ ثَوَابًا وَخَيْرٌ عُقْبًا (٤٤)
There, all authority belongs to Allah, the Real. He is best in rewarding, and best in requiting. (44)

Key Points

The Quran has repeatedly emphasized that when man faces danger and material means fail, he turns to Allah and calls upon Him from the depths of his soul. When he is caught in the waves at sea, or when punishment comes, he expresses faith. Here too, when that person faced his burnt garden and had no helper, he understood that true guardianship (Wilayah) belongs exclusively to the One God. «هنالك الولایة للّه الحقّ» The word Wilayah (ولایت) in this Ayah means power and succor (Nusrah).

Messages

1- The consequence of relying on other than Allah is failure. «لم تكن له فئة ینصرونه»

2- When the wrath of Allah arrives, wealth and children, no matter how numerous, are of no use. «ولم تكن له فئة ینصرونه» (There is no news of the companions of the time of prosperity when danger strikes.)

3- The resolution for all bitter events is only one sentence: that power belongs solely to Allah. «هنالك الولایة للّه الحقّ»

4- Non-Divine guardianships (Wilayahs) are unstable and false, and only the Wilayah of Allah is constant and true. «الولایة للّه الحق»

5- Whether we are calculating or far-sighted, we must turn to Allah. «هو خیرٌ ثواباً و خیرٌ عقباً»

6- Divine succor and the truthfulness of Allah guarantee the best reward and outcome. «هنالك الولایة للّه الحق هو خیر ثوابا و خیر عقبا»

7- The criterion for values is a good outcome and reward. «خیر ثوابا و خیر عقبا»

Ayah

وَٱضْرِبْ لَهُم مَّثَلَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا كَمَآءٍ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَٱخْتَلَطَ بِهِۦ نَبَاتُ ٱلْأَرْضِ فَأَصْبَحَ هَشِيمًا تَذْرُوهُ ٱلرِّيَـٰحُ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ مُّقْتَدِرًا (٤٥)
Draw for them the parable of the life of this world: [It is] like the water We send down from the sky. Then the earth’s vegetation mingles with it. Then it becomes chaff, scattered by the wind. And Allah has power over all things. (45)

Key Points

The verse speaks of the growth and greenery of the earth, the arrival of Divine wrath, and their burning and turning to ashes, so that it may be a lesson for people who are arrogant and heedless of Allah. (39) Yes, the world is like a rootless plant that turns green with a little rain and dries up with a little wind. Of course, what remains is the action (deed) of man. As we read in the Divan attributed to Amir al-Mu'minin عليه السلام: یا مَن بدنیاه اشتَغل قد غَرّه طُول الأمل

39) A verse similar to this one appears in Ayah 24 of Surah Yunus with more detail, and in Ayah 20 of Surah Al-Hadid.

Messages

1- The Prophet of Allah is commanded to use parables to explain truths and guide people. «واضرب لهم مثل الحیاة الدنیا»

2- One of the principles of education and propagation is the use of parables that are generally understandable and natural. «واضرب لهم مثل... كماء انزلناه...»

3- Every spring has an autumn, and every union has a separation. «نبات... هشیماً»

4- Let us not be pleased or deceived by the fleeting manifestations of the world, but rather think about the lasting future. «فاصبح هَشیماً تَذْروه الرّیاح»

Ayah

ٱلْمَالُ وَٱلْبَنُونَ زِينَةُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَٱلْبَـٰقِيَـٰتُ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتُ خَيْرٌ عِندَ رَبِّكَ ثَوَابًا وَخَيْرٌ أَمَلًا (٤٦)
Wealth and children are an adornment of the life of the world, but lasting righteous deeds are better with your Lord in reward and better in hope. (46)

Key Points

In narrations (Riwāyāt), examples for "al-Bāqiyāt al-Ṣāliḥāt" (the enduring good deeds) have been mentioned, such as the five daily prayers (40), the remembrance of Allah (41), and love for the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام (42). However, these are specific instances (Maṣādīq) that do not restrict the concepts of the Quran solely to themselves. All material blessings are adornments of the world, but wealth (māl) and children have a greater manifestation, and for this reason, the names of these two have been highlighted from among the countless Divine blessings. And since sons (banūn) are generally the active economic force, not daughters (banāt), therefore, "banūn" (sons) is mentioned alongside "māl" (wealth), not "banāt" (daughters). «المال و البنون زینة الحیاة الدنیا»

40) Tafsir Burhan. 41) Bihar, Vol. 74, p. 108. 42) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The manifestation of wealth and children is only in this world; in the Hereafter, wealth and children will be of no benefit. «زینة الحیاة الدنیا»

2- Those who possess material means should not become arrogant. And those who do not possess them should not despair. «المال والبنون زینة... والباقیات الصالحات خیر»

3- Wealth and children are not permanent; we should contemplate and attach our hearts to that which endures. «الباقیات الصالحات خیر»

4- In the presence of Allah, no good deed is erased, and the rewards are guaranteed. «والباقیات الصالحات خیر عند ربّك ثوابا»

5- Not everyone knows the value of good deeds. Most people perceive goodness and value in material enjoyments. «خیرٌ عند ربّك»

6- Reward and punishment are necessities of nurturing (Tarbiyah) and among the affairs of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «عند ربّك»

7- Do not think only of the reward; also contemplate the enduring future. «خیر عند ربّك ثواباً و خیر اَملاً»

8- In guidance (Irshād) and nurturing (Tarbiyah), we should utilize people's hopes and aspirations. «خیر عند ربّك ثواباً و خیر اَملا»

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ نُسَيِّرُ ٱلْجِبَالَ وَتَرَى ٱلْأَرْضَ بَارِزَةً وَحَشَرْنَـٰهُمْ فَلَمْ نُغَادِرْ مِنْهُمْ أَحَدًا (٤۷)
The day We shall set the mountains moving and you will see the earth in full view, We shall muster them, and We will not leave out anyone of them. (47)

Key Points

The Quran describes the state of the mountains on the verge of the establishment of the Resurrection in various ways, such as: 1- Earthquake and the shaking of the earth and the mountains. «تَرجُف الارض والجبال»(43) 2- Movement and displacement. «نُسیّر الجبال» 3- Being broken into pieces and turning into dust/sand. «بُسّت الجبال بَسّاً فكانت هَباءً مُنبثاً»(44)

43) Al-Muzzammil, 14. 44) Al-Waqi’ah, 5 - 6.

Messages

1- To avoid becoming attached to the life of this world, remembering the Resurrection is necessary. «ویوم...»

2- Good deeds are useful in many places, including the Day of Resurrection when even the mountains are set in motion. «و یوم نسیّر الجبال»(45)

3- In the land of Resurrection and the plains of the Gathering, there will be no lowlands or highlands. «ترى الارض بارزةً»

4- The Gathering and the Resurrection are certain. «حشرنا» (The past tense verb is used for the future.)

5- The Resurrection is for everyone, and there is no exception involved. «فلم نغادر منهم احداً» 45) Based on the use of the word «واو» (waaw) and «یوم» (yawm), which convey the concept of circumstance (zarfiyyah) in relation to the previous Ayah.

Ayah

وَعُرِضُوا۟ عَلَىٰ رَبِّكَ صَفًّا لَّقَدْ جِئْتُمُونَا كَمَا خَلَقْنَـٰكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍۭ ۚ بَلْ زَعَمْتُمْ أَلَّن نَّجْعَلَ لَكُم مَّوْعِدًا (٤۸)
They will be presented before your Lord in ranks: ‘Certainly you have come to Us just as We created you the first time. But you maintained that We shall not appoint a tryst for you.’ (48)

Key Points

Ṣaff (the row/line) is a sign of order and organization, and a symbol of respect and humility. In the Quran, the term ṣaff is used to describe the characteristics of the mujahidin (striving ones) and also the angels, and two Surahs of the Quran are named As-Saff and Aṣ-Ṣāffāt. If, in this world, armed forces are presented to their leaders with a specific order, then in the Hereafter, all human beings will inevitably and systematically be presented before Allah.

Messages

1- Presence in the Hereafter and presentation before the Lord is certain. «وعُرِضوا على ربّك»

2- Worldly classes will vanish in the Hereafter, and all people, rich and poor, will stand in one line. «صَفّاً»

3- Order is a value that is relevant both in this world and the Hereafter, both in society and in worship. «عُرِضوا... صَفّاً»

4- The manner of presence in the Hereafter is like the time of birth and presence in this world: helpless, weeping, and naked.(46) «كما خلقناكم اوّل مَرّة»

5- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical. «جئتمونا كما خلقناكم...»

6- The renewed creation in the Hereafter is just like the creation in this world. «كما خلقناكم اوّل مَرّة»

7- The disbelievers have no justification for denying the Resurrection. «بل زَعَمتم ألَّن نجعل لكم موعداً» (46) The supplications (munājāt) also refer to the nakedness at the time of the Resurrection and the Gathering (Maḥshar).

Ayah

وَوُضِعَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ فَتَرَى ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ مُشْفِقِينَ مِمَّا فِيهِ وَيَقُولُونَ يَـٰوَيْلَتَنَا مَالِ هَـٰذَا ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَا يُغَادِرُ صَغِيرَةً وَلَا كَبِيرَةً إِلَّآ أَحْصَىٰهَا ۚ وَوَجَدُوا۟ مَا عَمِلُوا۟ حَاضِرًا ۗ وَلَا يَظْلِمُ رَبُّكَ أَحَدًا (٤۹)
The Book will be set up. Then you will see the guilty apprehensive of what is in it. They will say, ‘Woe to us! What a book is this! It omits nothing, big or small, without enumerating it.’ They will find present whatever they had done, and your Lord does not wrong anyone. (49)

Key Points

The Quran has repeatedly spoken about the issue of the book, the file, and the record of deeds on the Day of Judgment, and states: Our messengers write: «اِنّ رُسلنا یَكتبون»(47), We also write the effects of their deeds: «نَكتُب ما قدّموا و آثارهم»(48), Their record of deeds is hung around their necks: «اَلزمناه طائره فى عُنُقه»(49), The files will be opened on the Day of Judgment: «و اذ الصُّحف نُشرت»(50), The records will be given into the hands of the individuals themselves; the record of the righteous will be given into their right hand, and the record of the wicked into their left hand. Just as every individual has a file, every nation also has a book and a file. «كلّ اُمّة تُدعى الى كتابها»(51)

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, upon returning from the Battle of Hunayn, stopped in a dry desert. He told His companions: Go around and gather whatever wood and dry brush you find. The companions went, and each brought something. They piled it up, and it accumulated. The Prophet said: «هكذا تجتمع الذنوب، ایّاكم و المحقّرات من الذنوب» Sins accumulate in this way, so beware of minor sins.(52)

From this event, we can also derive the lesson that the Prophet of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله provided His companions with practical, visual, demonstrative, and field-based training, combined with filling their leisure time, especially when the danger of arrogance resulting from victory in war was lurking, and they might have considered themselves sinless or having few sins.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: On the Day of Judgment, when a person's record of deeds is given to him, after looking at it and seeing that all his moments, words, movements, and actions have been recorded, he remembers all of them, as if he had committed them just an hour before. Therefore, he says: «یا ویلتنا ما لهذا الكتاب لایُغادر صغیرة...».(53)

Those who were careless and indifferent in this world and engaged in every kind of deed will experience fear and anxiety in the Hereafter, «مشفقین» (fearful), but the believers, who were committed and God-fearing in this world, will be at ease there. «اِنّا كنّا قبل فى اهلنا مشفقین»(54)

47) Yunus, 21. 48) Yasin, 12. 49) Al-Isra, 13. 50) At-Takwir, 10. 51) Al-Jathiyah, 28. 52) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 288. 53) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 54) At-Tur, 26.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, both the record of deeds of every individual will be given into his hand, and a Book will be placed before everyone. «و وُضِع الكتاب»

2- The anxiety and fear of the criminals stem from their own recorded actions, not from Allah. «مشفقین ممّا فیه»

3- The Day of Judgment is a day of regret and sorrow for the criminals. «یا ویلتنا»

4- The knowledge individuals have of their own actions on the Day of Judgment is such that it is as if everyone becomes literate, because the Book and reading are relevant to all. «وُضِع الكتاب... مشفقین ممّا فیه»

5- Human deeds have degrees (of size/importance). «صغیرةً ولا كبیرةً»

6- The Day of Judgment is the scene of the embodiment of human deeds. «وجدوا ما عملوا حاضراً»

7- Possessing justice and refraining from oppression are prerequisites of Divine Lordship. «و لایَظلم ربّك احداً»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ ٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِـَٔادَمَ فَسَجَدُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ إِبْلِيسَ كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْجِنِّ فَفَسَقَ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِۦٓ ۗ أَفَتَتَّخِذُونَهُۥ وَذُرِّيَّتَهُۥٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ مِن دُونِى وَهُمْ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّۢ ۚ بِئْسَ لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ بَدَلًا (٥٠)
When We said to the angels, ‘Prostrate before Adam,’ they prostrated, but not Iblis. He was one of the jinn, so he transgressed against his Lord’s command.
