Will you then take him and his offspring for guardians in My stead, though they are your enemies? How evil a substitute for the wrongdoers! (50)

Key Points

Prostration (Sajdah) is of three types: A: Prostration of worship (Sajdah al-Ibadah), which is exclusive to Allah, such as the prostration in prayer (Salat). B: Prostration of obedience (Sajdah al-Ita'ah), such as the prostration of the angels to Adam, which was done in obedience to the command of Allah. C: Prostration of greeting/respect (Sajdah al-Tahiyyah), honoring and thanking Allah, such as the prostration of Jacob upon the outcome concerning Joseph.

Messages

  1. The story of the angels' prostration to Adam and the rebellion of Iblis is worthy of contemplation and a source of lesson. «واذ قلنا...»

  2. The angels existed before mankind. «قلنا للملائكة اسجدوا لآدم»

  3. Man is superior to the angel, as he was made the object of their prostration. «اُسجدوا لآدم»

  4. The angels are obedient to the command of Allah. «اُسجدوا... فسجدوا»

  5. Those who do not prostrate are following the path of Iblis. «فسَجدوا الاّ ابلیس»

  6. One should not be arrogant about one's past record. Iblis, despite a long history of worship, became a transgressor (fasiq). «كان من الجنّ ففسق ...»

  7. Iblis has offspring and progeny. «و ذُریّته»

  8. Transgressors (fasiqan) should not be taken as friends. «ففسق ... أفتتّخذونه و ذریّته اولیاء»

  9. Followers of Satan must review their actions and consider what they are doing. «أفتتّخذونه و ذریّته اولیاء»

  10. Satan and his progeny are enemies of mankind. «وهم لكم عدوّ»

  11. Those who follow Satan instead of Allah are oppressors. «بئس للظّالمین بدلاً»

  12. Abandoning Allah and following Satan is choosing the worst substitute. «بئس للظّالمین بدلاً»

Ayah

۞ مَّآ أَشْهَدتُّهُمْ خَلْقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا خَلْقَ أَنفُسِهِمْ وَمَا كُنتُ مُتَّخِذَ ٱلْمُضِلِّينَ عَضُدًا (٥۱)
I did not make them witness to the creation of the heavens and the earth, nor to their own creation, nor do I take those who mislead others as assistants. (51)

Key Points

They said to Imam Ali عليه السلام: Keep Mu'awiyah fixed in governorship until your government is established, and dismiss him after gaining power! In response, the Imam said to them: «ما كنتُ متّخذ المضلّین عَضُدا» (I would not take those who lead astray as helpers/supporters.) (55) Allah is absolutely self-sufficient and needs no helper, even in creation. And if He entrusts matters to others, such as delegating administration to the angels, «و المدبّرات امراً» (By those who arrange [the] matter) (56), it is not out of weakness or inability, but out of wisdom and in the path of human training and growth.

55) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 3, p. 268. 56) An-Nazi'at, 5.

Messages

1- Allah has no need for a witness in creation. «ما اَشهدتهم»

2- No one knows the secrets of existence and creation except Allah. «ما اَشهدتهم خلق...»

3- The One who created has the right to guardianship (Wilayah). «أفتتّخذونه اولیاء... ما اشهدتهم خلق...»

4- Divine agents must be trustworthy (Amin). «ماكنت متخذّ المضلّین عضداً»

5- Those who are on the path and line of Allah should not seek help from the deviated, and they must maintain their political and economic independence. «ماكنت متّخِذَ المضلّین عضداً»

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ يَقُولُ نَادُوا۟ شُرَكَآءِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُمْ فَدَعَوْهُمْ فَلَمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا۟ لَهُمْ وَجَعَلْنَا بَيْنَهُم مَّوْبِقًا (٥۲)
The day He will say [to the polytheists], ‘Call those whom you maintained to be My partners,’ they will call them, but they will not respond to them, for We shall set an abyss between them. (52)

Key Points

Those who separate from God are bewildered and turn to everything, from humans to inanimate objects and animals, from the sun and moon in the sky to the cow and calf on the earth, from infallible angels to wicked devils. But whatever they call upon, they receive no answer, «فلم یستجیبوا لهم». But if they call upon God, their prayer will certainly be answered. «اُدعونى اَستَجب لكم»(57)

Question: We read in the Quran that Allah does not speak to those who conceal the truth and break covenants, «لایكلّمهم اللّه»(58). So why does He speak to the polytheists in this Ayah?

Answer: Perhaps this is because the scholars who conceal the truth are worse than the polytheists, or because the Day of Judgment has different stages. In one stage, Allah does not speak, but in another stage, He speaks to them and subjects them to reproach. It is also possible that the meaning is that He does not speak to them kindly, but He does speak words of rebuke and humiliation.

57) Ghafir, 60. 58) Al-Baqarah, 174; Al ‘Imran, 77.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, there will be a great distance between the polytheists and their deities. «نادوا»

2- On the Day of Judgment, Allah gives the polytheists the opportunity to seek help from their deities, but to what avail? «نادوا شركائى...فلم یستجیبوا لهم»

3- The root of polytheism (Shirk) is ignorance and baseless assumptions. «زعمتم»

Ayah

وَرَءَا ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ ٱلنَّارَ فَظَنُّوٓا۟ أَنَّهُم مُّوَاقِعُوهَا وَلَمْ يَجِدُوا۟ عَنْهَا مَصْرِفًا (٥۳)
The guilty will sight the Fire and know that they are about to fall in it, for they will find no way to escape it. (53)

Key Points

The criminals will have no escape route on the Day of Judgment, because salvation is either under the shadow of faith, or due to Divine forgiveness based on repentance and righteous deeds—from which their hands are empty—or intercession, which idols are incapable of providing. Therefore, Hellfire is certain for them.

Messages

  1. The place of destruction that was promised in the previous Ayah, mawbiqan (موبقا), is Hellfire. «رأى المجرمون النار»

  2. Shirk (polytheism) is a crime, and the polytheist is a criminal. «نادوا شركائى... رأى المجرمون»

  3. Seeing the Fire is a torment, and burning in it is another torment. «رأى المجرمون»

  4. On the Day of Judgment, although the criminals see the Fire from afar (59), it is so loud and roaring that they imagine they are falling into it right now. «فظنّوا أنّهم مواقعوها»

  5. Shirk results in eternal torment. «لم یجدوا عنها مَصرِفاً»

  6. The criminal on the Day of Judgment has no hope of salvation or escape route. «لم یجدوا عنها مَصرِفاً»

59) اذا رأتهم من مكان بعید، سمعوا لها تغیظاً و زمزاً». فرقان، ۱۲.

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنَا فِى هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ لِلنَّاسِ مِن كُلِّ مَثَلٍ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ أَكْثَرَ شَىْءٍ جَدَلًا (٥٤)
We have certainly interspersed this Quran with every kind of parable for the people. But man is the most disputatious of creatures. (54)

Key Points

«صرّفنا» means various forms of expression (or explanations). That is, We have spoken to them using every language and logic that had the potential for impact. «جدال» (Jidal/dispute) means conversation in the manner of conflict and seeking superiority. Alongside the positive aspects that man possesses—such as having the Divine Spirit, superiority over the angels and their prostration to him, and the power to penetrate and conquer existence, and the like—he also has many negative points which are mentioned in the verses of the Quran, such as: «ظلوماً» (extremely unjust), «جهولاً» (extremely ignorant), «اكثر شى ءٍ جدلاً» (most prone to dispute), «جزوعا» (impatient), «هلوعاً» (anxious/fretful), «مَنوعاً» (withholding/stingy), «لفى خسر» (surely in loss), «لیطغى» (surely transgresses), and...

Messages

1- Repetition, variety, and analogy are necessary and useful methods for guiding, leading, and educating people. «صرّفنا للنّاس من كلّ مثل»

2- The goal of the Quran is the guidance of all people, and the examples in the Quran are for the understanding of the general public. «صرّفنا للنّاس من كلّ مثل»

3- Allah has completed the proof (Hujjah) upon the people through the Quran. «لقد صرّفنا للناس»

4- In the Quran, there is an example for every principle of guidance and instruction. «كلّ مثل»

5- If the spirit of acceptance is absent in man, more examples will only lead him to more dispute. «كلّ مثل، اكثر شئىٍ جدلاً»

6- Man is a creature who seeks variety and is prone to argumentation. «صرّفنا... جدلا»

Ayah

وَمَا مَنَعَ ٱلنَّاسَ أَن يُؤْمِنُوٓا۟ إِذْ جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْهُدَىٰ وَيَسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ رَبَّهُمْ إِلَّآ أَن تَأْتِيَهُمْ سُنَّةُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ أَوْ يَأْتِيَهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ قُبُلًا (٥٥)
Nothing has kept these people from believing and pleading to their Lord for forgiveness when guidance came to them, except [their demand] that the precedent of the ancients come to pass for them, or that the punishment come to them, face to face. (55)

Key Points

The word «قُبل» (qubul) either means face-to-face and opposite, or it is the plural of «قبیل» (qabil) and refers to various types of punishment.

Messages

1- Refusing to believe, despite seeing all those signs, is a sign of human contentiousness. «جدلا و ما منع الناس»

2- Allah completes the proof (Hujjah) upon people so that no path or excuse for disbelief remains. «و ما منع النّاس»

3- Faith alone is not sufficient; seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) for lapses is also necessary. «یؤمنوا، یستغفروا»

4- The requirement of His Lordship (Rububiyyah) is keeping the path of repentance and seeking forgiveness open. «ویستغفروا ربّهم»

5- History has fixed traditions (Sunan) from which lessons must be learned. «سنّة الاوّلین»

6- Admonition and reasoning are not effective everywhere; sometimes punishment is necessary. «یأتیهم العذاب» Sometimes none of the factors of spiritual training, such as heavenly revelation, continuous propagation, observing history, and lessons, affect the stubborn person.

7- Faith and seeking forgiveness prevent divine punishment. «یؤمنوا... یستغفروا...یاتیهم العذاب»

8- Allah's hand is open for punishment, whether it is a punishment like that of the ancients or a new punishment. «سنّة الأوّلین او یأتیهم العذاب قبلا»

Ayah

وَمَا نُرْسِلُ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرِينَ وَمُنذِرِينَ ۚ وَيُجَـٰدِلُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِٱلْبَـٰطِلِ لِيُدْحِضُوا۟ بِهِ ٱلْحَقَّ ۖ وَٱتَّخَذُوٓا۟ ءَايَـٰتِى وَمَآ أُنذِرُوا۟ هُزُوًا (٥٦)
We do not send the apostles except as bearers of good news and as warners, but those who are faithless dispute fallaciously to refute thereby the truth, having taken My signs and what they are warned of in derision. (56)

Messages

1- Awareness of the Sunnah of Allah, the mission of the prophets, the reaction of the people, and the destiny of stubborn nations is a form of consolation for the Prophet. «ومانرسل المرسلین...»

2- The task of the prophets is to give glad tidings and warnings, not to compel people to accept. «الاّ مبشّرین و منذرین»

3- Allah completes the proof upon the people, «وما نرسل المرسلین ...» and people are not compelled to accept it. «الاّ مبشّرین و منذرین و یجادل الّذین كفروا...»

4- The goal of the disbelievers is to obliterate the truth, «لیدحضوا» and their tools are argumentation and mockery. «یجادل الّذین كفروا»

5- Conflict and argumentation between truth and falsehood have always existed. «یجادل» (The present tense verb signifies the continuity of the action.)

6- The disbelievers want to stop the truth using falsehood, «لیدحضوا به الحقّ» unaware that falsehood is bound to perish. «اِنّ الباطل كان زهوقا»(60)

7- The glad tidings and warnings of the prophets are realities that the disbelievers strive to obliterate. «مبشرین و منذرین... لیدحضوا به الحقّ»

8- Mocking the verses of Allah and the divine decrees is the work of the disbelievers. «واتخذّوا آیاتى و ما انذروا هزوا» (60) Al-Isra, 81.

Ayah

وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّن ذُكِّرَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِۦ فَأَعْرَضَ عَنْهَا وَنَسِىَ مَا قَدَّمَتْ يَدَاهُ ۚ إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ أَكِنَّةً أَن يَفْقَهُوهُ وَفِىٓ ءَاذَانِهِمْ وَقْرًا ۖ وَإِن تَدْعُهُمْ إِلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰ فَلَن يَهْتَدُوٓا۟ إِذًا أَبَدًا (٥۷)
Who is a greater wrongdoer than he who is reminded of the signs of his Lord, whereat he disregards them and forgets what his hands have sent ahead? Indeed We have cast veils on their hearts lest they should understand it, and a deafness into their ears; and if you invite them to guidance they will never [let themselves] be guided. (57)

Key Points

"Remembrance (Tadhakkur)" is the key indicating that man has found God deep within his soul and innate nature (Fitrah), but has become heedless of Him. Remembrance is for removing the veil of heedlessness. Imam Ali عليه السلام, in describing the Prophets, says: The goal of the Prophets' mission is to remind people to fulfill the forgotten things. "And remind them of His forgotten blessings." (و یذكّرهم منسىّ نعمته). (61)

"Akinna" (اَكنّة) is the plural of "Kinan" (كنان), meaning a veil or covering.

61) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 1.

Messages

1- Indifference to divine verses and failing to reflect upon them is the greatest injustice. (Referring to the words) "and who is more unjust," "was reminded," "turned away," "forgot." (و مَن اَظلَم، ذُكّر، اَعرِض، نَسى)

2- Remembering bad deeds paves the way for repentance and seeking forgiveness. Conversely, heedlessness of them causes the darkening of the soul and is the key to all misfortunes. (Referring to the phrase) "he forgot what his hands had sent forth." (نَسىَ ما قَدّمت یداه)

3- The punishment for indifference to divine verses, mockery, and heedlessness of sins is deprivation from understanding the truths. (Referring to the phrases) "so he turned away... We placed." (فاَعرض... جعلنا)

4- Divine punishment is not only in the Hereafter; taking away the power to comprehend truths is also a punishment. (Referring to the phrase) "coverings lest they understand it." (اَكنّةً أن یفقهوه)

5- In guiding people, mere proof and evidence are not enough; the acceptance of the audience is also necessary. (Referring to the phrase) "And if you invite them to guidance, they will never be guided." (و اِن تَدعُهم الى الهُدى فلن یهتدوا)

6- The path to guidance requires a soft heart and a listening ear. When these two are altered, guidance will not be accepted, even when invited by the Prophet himself. (Referring to the phrase) "they will never be guided, then, ever." (فلن یهتدوا اذاً ابداً)

Ayah

وَرَبُّكَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ذُو ٱلرَّحْمَةِ ۖ لَوْ يُؤَاخِذُهُم بِمَا كَسَبُوا۟ لَعَجَّلَ لَهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابَ ۚ بَل لَّهُم مَّوْعِدٌ لَّن يَجِدُوا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦ مَوْئِلًا (٥۸)
Your Lord is the All-forgiving dispenser of mercy. Were He to take them to task because of what they have committed, He would have surely hastened their punishment. But they have a tryst, [when] they will not find a refuge besides Him. (58)

Key Points

«مَوئِل» (Maw'il) means refuge and means of salvation. Allah is both Just and Forgiving (Ghafoor). According to His justice, He should punish the sinner, but due to His being the All-Forgiving (Ghaffār) and His mercy, He grants man respite to repent. However, if he does not repent, He holds him accountable through His justice.

Messages

1- Forgiveness and mercy are among the attributes of Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) and are necessary for nurturing (Tarbiyah). «ربّك الغفور ذوالرّحمة»

2- Allah is both the All-Forgiving and possesses vast mercy. «الغفور، ذو الرّحمة»

3- Divine wrath and punishment are the result of people's own deeds. «یؤاخذهم بماكسبوا»

4- Sins are uglier and their punishment is greater when a person commits them intentionally (with motive). «بماكسبوا»

5- The Divine tradition (Sunnah) is to grant respite to sinners. «بل لهم مَوعد»

6- Granting respite and delaying punishment are signs of Divine grace. «الغفور ذو الرّحمة... لهم موعدٌ»

7- Sinners should not be deceived by the Divine respites, because the appointed time will end, and the turn of wrath will arrive, for which there is no way of salvation for them. «لن یجدوا من دونه مَوئلاً»

Ayah

وَتِلْكَ ٱلْقُرَىٰٓ أَهْلَكْنَـٰهُمْ لَمَّا ظَلَمُوا۟ وَجَعَلْنَا لِمَهْلِكِهِم مَّوْعِدًا (٥۹)
Those are the towns that We destroyed when they were wrongdoers, and We appointed a tryst for their destruction. (59)

Messages

1- Lessons must be learned from history and the fate of previous generations. «تلك القرى اهلكناهم»

2- The secret to the misery and destruction of people is their own oppressive actions. «لمّا ظلموا»

3- The decline of civilizations and societies is under the Divine Will. «اهلكناهم لمّا ظلموا»

4- Divine wrath is based on wisdom and has a specific time appointed. «موعداً»

5- If you see oppressors living in comfort and ease, do not despair; they too have a respite. «جعلنا لمهلكم موعداً»

Ayahs

وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِفَتَىٰهُ لَآ أَبْرَحُ حَتَّىٰٓ أَبْلُغَ مَجْمَعَ ٱلْبَحْرَيْنِ أَوْ أَمْضِىَ حُقُبًا (٦٠)
When Moses said to his lad, ‘I will go on [journeying] until I have reached the confluence of the two seas, or have spent a long time [travelling].’ (60)
فَلَمَّا بَلَغَا مَجْمَعَ بَيْنِهِمَا نَسِيَا حُوتَهُمَا فَٱتَّخَذَ سَبِيلَهُۥ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ سَرَبًا (٦۱)
So when they reached the confluence between them, they forgot their fish, which found its way into the sea, sneaking away. (61)

Key Points

The word "Musa" (Moses) appears 136 times in the Quran, and in all instances, it refers to the same Prophet of firm resolve (Ulul-'Azm). The use of the word "fatā" (فَتى), which means youth or chivalrous young man, instead of ghulām (servant/boy) or pisar (son), is a sign of courtesy, kindness, and good naming. The intended meaning of "fatāh" (فتاه) in the Ayah is "Yusha' ibn Nun," who was the companion and attendant of Prophet Musa during that journey.(62) "Huqub" (حُقُب) means long years, 70 to 80 years.

The story of Musa and Khidr has been narrated in exegeses and traditions. For instance, it is mentioned in the book Sahih al-Bukhari, narrated by Ibn Abbas from Ubayy ibn Ka'b, that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: One day, while Prophet Musa was delivering a sermon, the Children of Israel asked him who was the most knowledgeable person. Musa said: I am. Allah reproached Musa, asking why He did not say: Allah knows best. Khidr is more knowledgeable than you. Musa asked: Where is Khidr? The address came: He is in the region of Majma' al-Bahrain (the junction of the two seas), and the sign of it is that you should place a fish in a basket and travel; wherever you lose the fish, that is the appointed place for meeting Khidr.(63)

Musa placed the fish in the basket and told his companion: Wherever you lose the fish, inform me. Musa and his companion traveled until they reached a sea and stopped beside a rock to rest. While Musa was asleep and his companion was awake, the fish moved and jumped out of the basket into the sea! Since Musa's companion saw him sleeping, he did not call him. After Musa woke up, he also forgot to inform him, and both continued on their way. After a day and a night, Musa said: Bring us our food, for we have become weary from this journey. Musa's companion recounted the story of the fish coming back to life and jumping into the water. Musa said: We must return to the very area where the fish jumped into the water, for that is our appointed meeting place.

Imam Baqir and Imam Sadiq علیهما السلام said: Musa's companion took the salted fish to the sea to wash it; the fish began to move in his hand and went into the sea.(64)

Some have considered Majma' al-Bahrain (the junction of the two seas) to be the confluence of two seas of prophethood (Musa and Khidr); Musa is the sea of apparent wisdom, and Khidr is the sea of inner wisdom.

Since the Prophets are infallible and do not forget, the meaning of the "forgetting" (nisyān) of the fish in the Ayah is setting the fish aside and abandoning it. This is similar to Ayahs that attribute nisyān (forgetting/neglect) to Allah, such as: «الیوم نَنساكم»(65) and «انّا نسیناكم»(66). Furthermore, those two great figures did not forget the fish; rather, they had taken it with them. And according to what we narrated, Musa's companion waited because he did not want to wake the Prophet from his sleep, and after waking up, he forgot to recount the incident.

In the Quran, animals have often served as signs, sources of inspiration, or messengers, such as the manner of Habil's burial inspired by the raven, the news of the disbelief of the people of Saba conveyed by the hoopoe, the revival of the fish in the story of the meeting of these two Prophets, the role of the spider in protecting the Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله in the cave, and the protective role of the dog for the Companions of the Cave (Aṣḥāb al-Kahf).

62) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 63) Allamah Sha'rani, in the margin of Tafsir Abu al-Futuh, considers the meeting place to be around Syria and Palestine. 64) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 65) Jathiyah, 64. 66) Sajdah, 14.

Messages

1- Musa's effort to meet Khidr and acquire knowledge is noteworthy. «واذ...»

2- When traveling, we should have a companion and fellow traveler. «قال موسى لفتاه»

3- Choosing a beautiful title (for a person) is recommended in Islam. «قال... لفتاه»

4- Migration and travel for the acquisition of knowledge have a very long history. «لاابرح...»

5- The seeker of knowledge must go to the scholar, not wait for the scholar to come. «لاابرح» means: I will not cease searching until I reach the scholar.

6- In the pursuit of knowledge and the scholar, it is appropriate if we search for a lifetime. «اَمضىَ حُقُبا»

7- The acquisition of knowledge has no end or limit. Even a Prophet like Musa must prepare for a long journey to acquire knowledge. «امضىَ حُقُبا»

8- A chivalrous companion is one who stays with a person until the end of the journey. «حتّى اَبلُغ مجمع البحرین ... فلمّا بلغا مجمع بینهما»

9- The sea and the seaside can be a suitable, safe, and ready location for teaching and spiritual meetings. «فلمّا بلغا مجمع بینهما»

Ayahs

فَلَمَّا جَاوَزَا قَالَ لِفَتَىٰهُ ءَاتِنَا غَدَآءَنَا لَقَدْ لَقِينَا مِن سَفَرِنَا هَـٰذَا نَصَبًا (٦۲)
So when they had passed on, he said to his lad, ‘Bring us our meal. We have certainly encountered much fatigue on this journey of ours.’ (62)
قَالَ أَرَءَيْتَ إِذْ أَوَيْنَآ إِلَى ٱلصَّخْرَةِ فَإِنِّى نَسِيتُ ٱلْحُوتَ وَمَآ أَنسَىٰنِيهُ إِلَّا ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ أَنْ أَذْكُرَهُۥ ۚ وَٱتَّخَذَ سَبِيلَهُۥ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ عَجَبًا (٦۳)
He said, ‘Did you see?! When we took shelter at the rock, indeed I forgot about the fish—and none but Satan made me forget to mention it!—and it made its way into the sea in an amazing manner!’ (63)

Key Points

Ghadā’ (غَداء) refers to the morning meal, and ‘Ashā’ (عَشاء) refers to the evening meal. According to Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, Prophet Moses complained to Allah about hunger on three occasions: First: After he brought water for the daughters of Shu'ayb and helped their sheep, he withdrew to a shade and said, "My Lord! I am needy of whatever good You may send down to me." (67) Second: In this Ayah. (68) Third: Where they asked the people of the village for food and they refused, he objected to Khidr, saying, "Why are you working for free for people who did not give us food?" (69)

The meeting place of Moses and Khidr had a sign, which was that the fish, which was the food of Moses and his companion, would leave the food container and go into the sea. When they reached the appointed place and the fish leaped into the sea, Moses' companion forgot to mention it to Moses. After they had walked a considerable distance, Moses' companion recounted the incident. They returned the extra distance they had walked and met Khidr at that very spot.

Prophet Moses عليه السلام had four journeys: 1- Safar Harab (Journey of Flight). «ففررت منكم لمّا خفتكم» (70) 2- Safar Talab (Journey of Seeking). «فلمّا أتاها نُودى من شاطئ الوادى الأيمن» (71) 3- Safar Tarab (Journey of Joy/Ecstasy). «ولمّا جاء موسى لمیقاتنا» (72) 4- Safar Ta'ab (Journey of Hardship). «لقد لقینا من سفرنا هذا نصباً» (73)

67) Qasas, 24. 68) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 69) Kahf, 77. 70) Shu'ara, 21. 71) Qasas, 30. 72) A'raf, 143. 73) Tafsir Kashf al-Asrar.

Messages

1- Among the etiquette of travel is having a companion and preparing food. «آتنا غداءنا» (74)

2- In the path of acquiring knowledge and gnosis and achieving goals, even the Prophets endured hardships. «لقینا من سفرنا هذا نصباً»

3- Satans are obstacles to the meeting and companionship of Moses and the Khidrs (i.e., the righteous guides), and for this reason, they caused Moses' companion to forget. «ما أنسانِيهُ إلاّ الشیطان» (74) Just as the People of the Cave also carried cash with them. «فابعثوا بورقكم هذه» (Kahf, 19).

Ayahs

قَالَ ذَٰلِكَ مَا كُنَّا نَبْغِ ۚ فَٱرْتَدَّا عَلَىٰٓ ءَاثَارِهِمَا قَصَصًا (٦٤)
He said, ‘That is what we were after!’ So they returned, retracing their footsteps. (64)
فَوَجَدَا عَبْدًا مِّنْ عِبَادِنَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ رَحْمَةً مِّنْ عِندِنَا وَعَلَّمْنَـٰهُ مِن لَّدُنَّا عِلْمًا (٦٥)
[There] they found one of Our servants whom We had granted a mercy from Ourselves, and taught him a knowledge from Our own. (65)

Key Points

As Imam Sadiq عليه السلام stated: Moses was more knowledgeable than Khidr in the science of Sharia (religious law), but Khidr was more knowledgeable in a different field and mission other than that. (75) The intended meaning of "عَبد" (servant) in this Ayah is Prophet Khidr, who was a Prophet based on the following reasons:

A: Someone who becomes the teacher of a Prophet like Moses must certainly be one of the Prophets.

B: The expressions "عبدنا," "عبده," "عبادنا" (Our servant, His servant, Our servants) in the Quran are generally specific to the Prophets.

C: Khidr told Moses: All the extraordinary deeds you witnessed from me and could not bear patiently were all according to the command and will of Allah, not my own will. "ما فَعَلتُه من امرى"

D: Moses promised Khidr that he would not do anything against his command, "لا اَعصى لك امراً" (I will not disobey you in any matter), and someone to whom a Prophet of firm resolve (Ulul-'Azm) submits unconditionally must certainly be infallible (Ma'sum) and a Prophet.

E: Ilm Ladunni (knowledge from His presence) is specific to the Prophets. Allah said concerning Khidr: "علّمناه من لدنّا" (We taught him knowledge from Our presence).

F: Some commentators have also considered the meaning of "رَحمة" (mercy) to be prophethood. "آتیناه رحمة من عندنا" (We granted him mercy from Our presence).

75) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Some sciences are not acquired through practice, experience, and teaching, but require Ilm Ladunni (knowledge from His presence), such as the knowledge of the Prophets. "من لدنّا علما"

2- The ultimate result of hardship and searching is finding and reaching the goal. "فوجدا عبداً"

3- Guiding Khidrs are found in society, (76) the important thing is to search, find them, and become their student. "عبداً من عبادنا"

4- Whoever wishes to call people to the servitude of Allah must ensure that both he and his teacher are servants (of Allah). "من عبادنا"

5- Receiving Divine mercy and knowledge is achieved under the shade of servitude (to Allah). "عبداً من عبادنا آتیناه رحمة... وعلّمناه"

6- Above every knowledgeable person is one more knowledgeable; therefore, we should not become arrogant about our knowledge. "عبداً... علّمناه من لدنا علماً"

76) Do not traverse this stage without the companionship of Khidr; it is darkness, beware of the danger of going astray. (Hafez)

Ayah

قَالَ لَهُۥ مُوسَىٰ هَلْ أَتَّبِعُكَ عَلَىٰٓ أَن تُعَلِّمَنِ مِمَّا عُلِّمْتَ رُشْدًا (٦٦)
Moses said to him, ‘May I follow you for the purpose that you teach me some of the probity you have been taught?’ (66)

Key Points

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: When Moses met Khidr, a bird picked up a drop of water from the sea with its beak and dropped it onto the ground in front of them. Khidr said to Moses: Did you understand the secret of this bird's action? It teaches us that our knowledge, compared to the knowledge of Allah, is like a drop compared to an endless ocean. (77)

In these short verses, several points regarding etiquette and humility towards the teacher are observed, including: A: Moses began his discipleship with permission. «هل» B: He introduced himself as a follower of the teacher. «اتبعّك» C: He introduced Khidr as the teacher. «تعلّمن» D: He considered himself a student of only a portion of the teacher's knowledge. «ممّا» E: He linked the teacher's knowledge to the unseen (Divine knowledge). «علّمت» F: He considered the teacher's instruction effective. «رشداً» G: Just as Allah taught you, you should also teach me. «ممّا عُلِّمت رشداً» H: He promised not to disobey. (Two verses later): «لا اعصى لك امراً» I: He considered the teacher's actions and words as commands. «لا اعصى لك امراً» J: He did not promise for the future and its stability, saying: «اِن شاء اللّه»

77) Tafsirs Al-Mizan, Namuneh, and Durr al-Manthur.

Messages

1- To receive knowledge, one must possess etiquette and humility before the teacher. «هل اتّبعك ...»

2- Humility towards scholars and teachers is among the ethics of the prophets. «هل اتّبعك ...»

3- Whoever is eager for knowledge and learning strives and moves forward. «هل اتّبعك»

4- Traveling with a scholar and enduring hardships on the path of acquiring knowledge and reaching growth and perfection is valuable. «هل اتّبعك»

5- The seeker of knowledge must have a goal and should not be merely impressed by personality. «على أن تُعلّمن»

6- Although Divine affairs sometimes proceed through miracles, the fundamental rule is to follow the natural course. Moses had to be a student to learn wisdom. «أن تُعلّمن»

7- Even the Prophets of Resolve (Ulul-'Azm) did not refrain from acquiring knowledge. «على أن تُعلّمن»

8- Hadrat Khidr was a prophet and possessed 'Ilm Ladunni (knowledge directly from God). «عُلِّمت»

9- Traversing the path of perfection and attaining special Divine knowledge requires a teacher and a guide. «تعلّمن ممّا عُلّمت»

10- The knowledge of the prophets is limited and subject to increase. «تُعلّمن ممّا...» (Even the most knowledgeable people of the time have scientific limitations.)

11- The ranks of the prophets in benefiting from knowledge and perfection are different. «تُعَلّمن ممّا...»

12- Possessing all levels of knowledge and perfection is not a prerequisite for prophethood. «تُعَلّمن»

13- Awareness of Divine knowledge and sciences guarantees human growth and perfection. «تُعلّمن ممّا عُلِّمت رُشدا»

14- Knowledge alone is not the goal; rather, it must be a source of growth, leading man toward righteous deeds and bringing humility, not arrogance and contention. (78) «رُشداً»

78) The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله used to recite in the ta'qib (supplications following) of his prayer: «أعوذ بك من علمٍ لاینفع» (I seek refuge in You from knowledge that is not beneficial). Bihar, Vol. 86, p. 18.

Ayahs

قَالَ إِنَّكَ لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِىَ صَبْرًا (٦۷)
He said, ‘Indeed you cannot have patience with me! (67)
وَكَيْفَ تَصْبِرُ عَلَىٰ مَا لَمْ تُحِطْ بِهِۦ خُبْرًا (٦۸)
And how can you have patience about something you do not comprehend?’ (68)
قَالَ سَتَجِدُنِىٓ إِن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ صَابِرًا وَلَآ أَعْصِى لَكَ أَمْرًا (٦۹)
He said, ‘You will find me, God willing, to be patient, and I will not disobey you in any matter.’ (69)
قَالَ فَإِنِ ٱتَّبَعْتَنِى فَلَا تَسْـَٔلْنِى عَن شَىْءٍ حَتَّىٰٓ أُحْدِثَ لَكَ مِنْهُ ذِكْرًا (۷٠)
He said, ‘If you follow me, do not question me concerning anything until I myself first mention it for you.’ (70)

Key Points

As we explained in the previous verses, although Prophet Moses (Musa) is one of the Prophets of Resolve (Ulul-'Azm) and is the most knowledgeable in the science of Sharia (Islamic Law), Khidr's knowledge was greater in other sciences.

Messages

1- The educator and teacher must be aware of the capacity of the students. «اِنّك لن تستطیع...»

2- When delegating cultural responsibilities, merits must be recognized and weaknesses must be pointed out. «اِنّك لن تستطیع...»

3- The capacity of individuals for knowledge differs; even Moses does not have the tolerance for Khidr's actions. «لن تستطیع»

4- Academic growth is not possible without patience. «رشداً ... و كیف تصبر»

5- Many disputes and resentments arise from people's lack of awareness regarding each other's thoughts and goals. «و كیف تصبر ...»

6- Awareness and scientific mastery increase a person's capacity and patience. «كیف تصبر على مالم تحط به خُبرا»

7- Through patience and divine grace, one can achieve growth and perfection. «ان شاءاللّه صابراً»

8- For every task we intend to perform in the future, we must not forget the divine will and should say «ان شاءاللّه» (if Allah wills). «ستجدنى ان شاء اللّه صابرا»

9- Khidr was infallible (Ma'sum), because Moses committed not to disobey his instructions. «لا اعصى لك امراً»

10- Obedience to the teacher and patience in the path of learning are the manners and conditions of acquiring knowledge. «ستجدنى... صابرا»

11- Taking commitments in academic and educational matters is permissible. «فان اتبعتنى فلا تسئلنى»

12- Asking questions has a time limit, and haste in questioning is forbidden. «فلا تسئلنى... حتّى احدث لك»

13- All actions of the Prophets are based on reason, logic, and wisdom. «احدث لك منه ذكراً»

14- The master and educator must remove ambiguity from the minds of the students. «حتى احدث لك منه ذكراً»

Ayahs

فَٱنطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا رَكِبَا فِى ٱلسَّفِينَةِ خَرَقَهَا ۖ قَالَ أَخَرَقْتَهَا لِتُغْرِقَ أَهْلَهَا لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْـًٔا إِمْرًا (۷۱)
So they went on and when they boarded the boat, he made a hole in it. He said, ‘Did you make a hole in it to drown its people? You have certainly done a monstrous thing!’ (71)
قَالَ أَلَمْ أَقُلْ إِنَّكَ لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِىَ صَبْرًا (۷۲)
He said, ‘Did I not say that you cannot have patience with me?’ (72)

Key Points

The term kharaq (خَرق) means tearing something out of malice/corruption and without deliberation. The word imr (اِمر) refers to a significant, astonishing, or extremely repulsive deed.

Messages

1- Do not waste time in learning. (Fanṭalaqā - فانطلقا) (The letter fā (ف) indicates immediacy.)

2- The companionship of individuals is sometimes not until the end of the journey. Moses' first companion was not with them for the rest of the way, and Moses and Khidr set off as two people. (Fanṭalaqā - فانطلقا) and He did not say: (Fanṭaliqū - فانطلقوا).

3- The acquisition of knowledge is not limited to a specific time, place, or means. (Rakabā fī as-safīnah - ركبا فى السفینة) (One can learn on the sea, aboard a ship, and even while traveling.)

4- If we accept someone's knowledge and wisdom, we must remain silent regarding their actions, even if they seem strange to us. (As-safīnatu kharaqahā - السفینة خرقها)

5- Sometimes, destruction is a prerequisite for instruction/learning. (Kharaqahā - خرقها)

6- In times of danger, everyone looks out for themselves and their own interests, but the Prophets think of others. (Li-tughriqa ahlahā - لتغرق اهلها) and He did not say: (Li-tughriqanā - لتغرقنا).

7- Tearing the ship, outwardly, was both an unauthorized interference with the property of others and an unjustified infliction of damage and loss upon the property and lives of themselves and others; hence, Prophet Moses objected. (Shay’an imrā - شیئاً اِمراً)

Ayahs

قَالَ لَا تُؤَاخِذْنِى بِمَا نَسِيتُ وَلَا تُرْهِقْنِى مِنْ أَمْرِى عُسْرًا (۷۳)
He said, ‘Do not take me to task for my forgetting, and do not be hard upon me.’ (73)
فَٱنطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا لَقِيَا غُلَـٰمًا فَقَتَلَهُۥ قَالَ أَقَتَلْتَ نَفْسًا زَكِيَّةًۢ بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ لَّقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْـًٔا نُّكْرًا (۷٤)
So they went on until they came upon a boy, whereat he slew him. He said, ‘Did you slay an innocent soul, without [his having slain] anyone? You have certainly done a dire thing!’ (74)

Key Points

Prophets are neither forgetful, nor is forgetfulness subject to criticism or accountability (Mū'ākhadhah). Therefore, in the phrase «لاتؤاخذنى بما نَسیتُ» (Do not hold me accountable for what I forgot), the intent of nisyān (forgetting) was abandoning the commitment to follow and remain silent, due to actions that seemed unlawful (contrary to Sharia) to Moses. «اِرهاق» (Irhaq) means both overwhelming with force and dominance, and assigning a difficult task. It is inferred from the words «غلام» (ghulām - boy/youth) and «زكیّة» (zakiyyah - pure/innocent) that the youth had not yet reached the age of religious obligation (Tuklīf). The word «نُكر» (nukr - heinous/repugnant) is stronger than «اِمر» (imr - serious/grievous), because regarding the sinking of the ship, He said: «شیئاً اِمراً» (a grievous thing), but regarding the killing of the youth, He said: «شیئاً نُكراً» (a repugnant thing).

Messages

1- The teacher or master may hold his student accountable. «لاتؤاخذنى» (Do not hold me accountable).

2- In teaching and training, the task should not be made difficult for the students. «لاترهقنى من امرى عسراً» (Do not impose upon me a difficulty in my affair).

3- The teacher or master should continue the instruction and guidance after the student acknowledges the mistake and apologizes. «فانطلقا» (So they both proceeded).

4- The teacher must gradually familiarize the student with knowledge and wisdom. «فانطلقا» (Moses becomes acquainted with the secrets step by step).

5- Observing the established principles (Muḥkamāt) is more important than ethical commitments. «أقتلتَ نفساً زكیّة» (Did you kill a pure soul?). Because Moses considered murder to be among the forbidden acts (munkarāt), he deemed Nahy ‘anil Munkar (forbidding evil) a necessary action, and thus forbade it, abandoning the ethical commitment he had made.

6- The law of retribution (Qisās) was also present in the religion of Moses. «أقتلتَ نفساً زكیّة بغیر نفس» (Did you kill a pure soul without [it being in exchange for] a soul?). Moses’ objection was: Why did you kill a youth who was not a murderer?

7- Sometimes what is considered "good" (ma‘rūf) by one person appears as "evil" (munkar) to another. «جئت شیئاً نُكراً» (You have certainly done a repugnant thing).

Ayahs

۞ قَالَ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكَ إِنَّكَ لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِىَ صَبْرًا (۷٥)
He said, ‘Did I not tell you that you cannot have patience with me?’ (75)
قَالَ إِن سَأَلْتُكَ عَن شَىْءٍۭ بَعْدَهَا فَلَا تُصَـٰحِبْنِى ۖ قَدْ بَلَغْتَ مِن لَّدُنِّى عُذْرًا (۷٦)
He said, ‘If I question you about anything after this, do not keep me in your company. You already have enough excuse on my part.’ (76)

Key Points

| The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: If Moses had been patient, he would have seen more wonders from Khidr. (79)

79) Tafsir Tabari and Abul Futuh.

Messages

1- The friends of Allah (Awliya Allah) are not all at the same level regarding their capacity and tolerance for knowledge. «لن تستطیع»

2- Three times of testing and granting respite is sufficient for recognizing character traits. «اِن سئلتك عن شى ء بعدها»

3- If we have intellectual conflict with someone and do not understand each other, we should separate without resistance. «فلا تصاحبنى»

4- Moses' objections were due to the ambiguities he saw in Khidr's actions and were in the nature of a question. «اِن سئلتك»

5- We should give a fair judgment regarding our own performance. «فلاتصاحبنى» Moses accepted responsibility for his lack of patience.

6- When separating from someone, observe courtesy. «فلا تصاحبنى قد بَلغتَ من لدنّى عُذرا»

7- Not every separation is a sign of malice, resentment, pride, or arrogance. «قدبَلغتَ من لدنّى عذراً»

Ayah

فَٱنطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَآ أَتَيَآ أَهْلَ قَرْيَةٍ ٱسْتَطْعَمَآ أَهْلَهَا فَأَبَوْا۟ أَن يُضَيِّفُوهُمَا فَوَجَدَا فِيهَا جِدَارًا يُرِيدُ أَن يَنقَضَّ فَأَقَامَهُۥ ۖ قَالَ لَوْ شِئْتَ لَتَّخَذْتَ عَلَيْهِ أَجْرًا (۷۷)
So they went on until they came to the people of a town. They asked its people for food, but they refused to extend them any hospitality. There they found a wall which was about to collapse, so he erected it. He said, ‘Had you wished, you could have taken a wage for it.’ (77)

Key Points

The people refused to give food to Moses and Khidr, but Khidr alone repaired the ruined wall for free.

Messages

1- Every educational period requires movement, and diversity of location is valuable for new learning. «فانطلقا»

2- A nation that suffers moral decline will refuse to give bread even to a Prophet of Resolve (Ulul-'Azm). «اَبَوا أن یُضیّفوهما» (Of course, this is assuming they recognized them.)

3- Those who do not host strange guests are worthy of blame. «فابَوا ...»

4- The indifference and unkindness of people should not negatively affect our work. «فابَوا أن یضیّفوهما»

5- Prophets sometimes lived in extreme need. «اِستطعما»

6- Although the needy may be content with the minimum, human dignity dictates that you host them to the extent of a proper feast/hospitality. «اِستطعما... یُضیّفوهما»

7- The guest does not always have to be familiar; one must also host the strange guest and the traveler (Ibn al-Sabil). «فابَوا أن یضیّفوهما»

8- Deficiencies must be corrected and repaired, not deemed worthless and abandoned simply because they are flawed. «فوجدا فیها جداراً ... فاقامه»

9- The Divine Saints (Awliya' Allah) are not vengeful or spiteful. Although the people did not host them, Khidr served them. «ابَوا أن یضیّفوهما - فاقامه»

10- Prophets have authority (Wilayah) over the property and lives of the people, allowing them to act as they deem fit without needing permission from the owner. «فاقامه»

11- The logic of "first livelihood, then work" and "first provision, then empowerment" is not always correct. The hungry Prophet works for free. «اَبَوا... فاقامه» When we see a need, we should get to work and not wait for an invitation, budget, colleague, or regulation. «جداراً، فاقامه»

12- Working for Allah without receiving wages is not a fault. «فاقامه»

13- When we determine a task to be useful and necessary, we should pay no attention to the criticism of others. «لتّخذتَ علیه اجراً»

14- Working, receiving wages, and being hired are not shameful. «لتّخذتَ علیه اجراً»

15- The Saints of Allah do not work for money. «لو شئتَ لتّخذتَ علیه اجراً»

Ayah

قَالَ هَـٰذَا فِرَاقُ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنِكَ ۚ سَأُنَبِّئُكَ بِتَأْوِيلِ مَا لَمْ تَسْتَطِع عَّلَيْهِ صَبْرًا (۷۸)
He said, ‘This is where you and I shall part. I will inform you about the interpretation of that over which you could not maintain patience. (78)

Key Points

Ta'wil (interpretation/inner meaning) means referring back or returning, and it is applied to any action that reaches its goal or unveils secrets. The separation of Khidr from Moses was at Moses' own suggestion, who said: «اِن سألتك عن شى ء بعدها فلاتصاحبنى» (If I question you about anything after this, do not keep me company), and he prepared the ground for it through repeated questions and objections, and by leaving him alone while building the wall. Moses was asked: What was the hardest moment you ever experienced? He said: Although I heard slanders from the Pharaohites and saw excuses from the Children of Israel, the most difficult time was the moment Khidr said: «هذا فراق بینى و بینك» (This is the parting between me and you). (80)

Let me weep, like a cloud in spring, For a lament rises even from stone, On the day of bidding farewell to companions.

80) Mahajjat al-Bayda' and Tafsir Abu al-Futuh.

Messages

1- Whoever does not appreciate the value of union will suffer separation. «هذا فراق...»

2- We should not lose our companions over the slightest criticism. Khidr said: «هذا فراق...» only after several stages.

3- Resolve the mental ambiguities of others as quickly as possible. «سأنبّئك»

4- Increase people's capacity by teaching wisdom. «سأنبّئك بتأویل مالم تستطع...»

5- Apparent events sometimes have an inner, hidden aspect. «تأویل مالم تستطع علیه صبراً»

Ayah

أَمَّا ٱلسَّفِينَةُ فَكَانَتْ لِمَسَـٰكِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ فَأَرَدتُّ أَنْ أَعِيبَهَا وَكَانَ وَرَآءَهُم مَّلِكٌ يَأْخُذُ كُلَّ سَفِينَةٍ غَصْبًا (۷۹)
As for the boat, it belonged to some poor people who work on the sea. I wanted to make it defective, for behind them was a king seizing every ship usurpingly. (79)

Key Points

Waraa’ (وراء) refers to any hidden or concealed place, whether that place is in front of a person or behind him. What a person observes is the apparent face of matters, which may very well have an inner face as well. The apparent actions of Khidr, in the view of Prophet Moses, were wrong, but hidden within them was a secret, a mystery, and a truth. Khidr did not pierce the ship in such a way that water would enter and cause it to sink; rather, He merely rendered it defective. How many defects and flaws there are that contain underlying benefits (81). Rendering the ship defective was so that it would not fall into the hands of that tyrannical king and those poor people would not become poorer. In reality, it was the repulsion of the greater evil (al-afsad) by means of the lesser evil (al-fasid), which is not the work of everyone, and discerning the more important from the important is the task of profound religious scholars. The Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام sometimes criticized and found fault with some of their sincere companions in the presence of others, so that they would not fall under the suspicion of the tyrannical government and their lives would remain safe. Just as Imam Sadiq علیه السلام publicly criticized Zurarah so that he would be safe from the persecution of the Abbasids, He then sent him a message saying, "I did this to protect your life." He then recited this Ayah: «اما السفینة...» and said: "You are the best ship in this sea, and the tyrant is pursuing you and watching you." (82) Those who step into the world of secrets and become aware of the inner reality of things are those who have passed beyond the stage of the apparent, just like Moses علیه السلام, who was knowledgeable of the Sharia and acted upon it, and during his companionship with Khidr, he also became aware of the inner secrets.

81) How many failures lead to things being set right; the key to the beggar's sustenance is a lame foot and a weak hand (Sa'ib). 82) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Observing order in the method of teaching is important. The first question was about damaging the ship, so the answer to this very question is presented first. «امّا السفینة»

2- Untold secrets and the mystery of actions will one day be revealed. «امّا السفینة»

3- A wise person never performs a futile act; His actions are based on wisdom and expediency (83). «امّا السفینة»

4- Committed individuals must protect the rights and properties of the deprived. «فكانت لمساكین...»

5- The friends of Allah are compassionate towards the needy (masakin) and the deprived. «لمساكین»

6- It is possible that some people, despite having capital and work, still do not earn enough for their expenses and are considered miskin (needy) according to the Quran. «مساكین یعملون»

7- Partnership in capital and labor is a legal contract and existed in previous religions as well. «مساكین یعملون»

8- Usurping rulers must be prevented from gaining access to people's property. «اعیبها... ملك یأخذ»

9- Repelling the greater evil (al-afsad) by means of the lesser evil (al-fasid) is permissible, and observing the more important and the important is necessary. «اعیبها... ملك یأخذ»

10- Tyrants (Taghuts) are not satisfied with little. They show no mercy even to the poor and those with small capital. «یأخذ كل سفینةٍ»

11- Although Khidr's action was by the command of Allah, «ما فعلتُه عن أمرى» (I did not do it of my own accord), yet out of respect (adab), he attributed the act of rendering the ship defective to himself. «أعیبها»

12- The work of the prophets is usually based on apparent laws, but Khidr's duty was based on underlying realities. «اردت ان اعیبها... ملك یأخذ»

83) If Khidr broke the ship in the sea, a hundred rectifications exist within Khidr's breaking (Rumi/Mawlawi).

Ayahs

وَأَمَّا ٱلْغُلَـٰمُ فَكَانَ أَبَوَاهُ مُؤْمِنَيْنِ فَخَشِينَآ أَن يُرْهِقَهُمَا طُغْيَـٰنًا وَكُفْرًا (۸٠)
As for the boy, his parents were faithful [persons], and We feared he would overwhelm them with rebellion and unfaith. (80)
فَأَرَدْنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَهُمَا رَبُّهُمَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهُ زَكَوٰةً وَأَقْرَبَ رُحْمًا (۸۱)
So We desired that their Lord should give them in exchange one better than him in respect of purity and closer in mercy. (81)

Key Points

Irhaq (إرهاق) means compelling someone else to do a task whose performance is difficult and exhausting for them. It is mentioned in a hadith: Allah gave those two believers a daughter instead of that boy, and from her lineage, seventy prophets emerged. (84) Sometimes a child causes the deviation and disbelief (kufr) of the parents, [as stated in the verse] «خشینا أن یُرهقهما...» (We feared that he would overburden them...). And sometimes the parents drag the child's pure, monotheistic nature (fitra) toward disbelief and deviant religions. Just as the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Every child is inherently God-knowing and a believer by nature (fitra), unless his parents change him. (85)

84) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 85) «كلّ مولودٍ یولد على الفطرة حتّى یكون ابواه یهوّدانه و ینصّرانه». Bihar, Vol. 3, p. 282.

Messages

1- The friends of Allah (Awliya Allah) do not perform vain or useless acts, even if they appear contrary [to convention] outwardly. «اما الغلام» (As for the boy)

2- The death of a child is better than him having a bad end and causing corruption and transgression. «یرهقهما طغیاناً و كفراً» (he would overburden them with transgression and disbelief)

3- Allah has special regard for believing parents. «ابواه مؤمنین فخشینا» (his parents were believers, and we feared)

4- Sometimes the body must be sacrificed for the sake of the ideology (faith). The child is killed so that the faith of the parents remains preserved. «خشینا ان یرهقهما» (We feared that he would overburden them)

5- Trials (fitan) must be anticipated and eradicated at the root. «خشینا ان یرهقهما» (We feared that he would overburden them)

6- Transgression and rebellion are the groundwork for disbelief (kufr). (Transgression [طغیان] precedes Disbelief [كفر] in the verse.)

7- The will of the men of Allah is the Will of Allah. «اردنا ان یبدِلهما» (We intended that He should replace them)

8- The friends of Allah (Awliya Allah) have responsibility and are also concerned about the future of the people. «اردنا ان یبدِلهما» (We intended that He should replace them)

9- Sometimes the reason believers face certain misfortunes is to preserve their own faith and belief. «یبدِلَهما خیراً منه» (He should replace him with something better than him)

10- If Allah takes something away from a believer, He gives him something better than it. «یبدِلهما ربّهما خیراً منه» (their Lord should replace him with something better than him)

11- The removal of an unworthy child and his replacement with a righteous child is a manifestation of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah). «یبدِلَهما ربّهما خیراً منه» (their Lord should replace him with something better than him)

12- The purity and righteousness of children alone are not enough; they must also be kind toward their parents. «زكوة واقرب رحماً» (purity and nearer to mercy/affection)

13- What holds value is the child's soundness, purity, and affection toward the parents, not whether the child is a girl or a boy. «زكوةً و اقرب رحماً» (purity and nearer to mercy/affection)

Ayah

وَأَمَّا ٱلْجِدَارُ فَكَانَ لِغُلَـٰمَيْنِ يَتِيمَيْنِ فِى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ وَكَانَ تَحْتَهُۥ كَنزٌ لَّهُمَا وَكَانَ أَبُوهُمَا صَـٰلِحًا فَأَرَادَ رَبُّكَ أَن يَبْلُغَآ أَشُدَّهُمَا وَيَسْتَخْرِجَا كَنزَهُمَا رَحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ ۚ وَمَا فَعَلْتُهُۥ عَنْ أَمْرِى ۚ ذَٰلِكَ تَأْوِيلُ مَا لَمْ تَسْطِع عَّلَيْهِ صَبْرًا (۸۲)
As for the wall, it belonged to two boy orphans in the city. Under it there was a treasure belonging to them. Their father had been a righteous man. So your Lord desired that they should come of age and take out their treasure—as a mercy from your Lord. I did not do that out of my own accord. This is the interpretation of that over which you could not maintain patience.’ (82)

Key Points

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said in a narration: That treasure was wise words written on a golden tablet. The content of those wisdoms was this: Astonishment at one who believes in Divine destiny, why does he grieve!? Wonder at one who is certain of death, why is he happy; certain of accountability, why is he heedless; certain of provision (rizq), why does he put himself through excessive hardship; and certain of the world's changeability, why does he trust it!?(86)

According to the narrations (ahadith), Allah's grace and mercy are not exclusive to immediate children, but also have an effect on subsequent generations, and the good deeds of ancestors cause Allah's favor upon their progeny.(87)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Allah revealed to Moses: I reward the children because of the goodness of the fathers. Then He said: If you betray the honor of people, your honor will be betrayed. «كما تَدین تدان» (As you judge, you shall be judged).(88)

In one instance, a child is killed for the sake of preserving the parents' faith, and in another instance, because the father was righteous, a prophet works as a laborer so that capital reaches the child.

When some people objected to Imam Mujtaba عليه السلام regarding the peace treaty with Mu'awiyah, he said: Do you not know that Khidr, for the sake of expediency (maslahat), holed the ship and killed the youth, and his actions incurred the wrath of Moses? O people! If I had not accepted the peace treaty, all the Shi'a on earth would have been destroyed. «لولا ما اَتیتُ لما ترك من شیعتنا على وجه الارض احدٌ الاّ قُتل».(89)

«قریة» (Qaryah) and «مدینة» (Madinah) are both of the same meaning. Just as in Ayah 77 He said: «اَتَیا اهل قریة» (They came to the people of a town), here He says: «فى المدینة» (in the city).(90)

«اَشُدّ» (Ashudd) is the plural of «شدّة» (shiddah), and «یبلغا اشُدّهما» means until they reach their full maturity/growth.

86) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 87) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 88) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 89) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 90) According to our learned friend, Mr. Kalbasi: If we examine the words "Qaryah" and "Madinah" in the Quran, we will observe that "Madinah" is used in cases where the light of guidance has shone, and wherever the light of guidance is absent, "Qaryah" is used.

Messages

1- An immature child can also be an owner, but the condition for disposing of assets is capability and maturity. «كنز لهما، حتّى یبلغا اشدّهما»

2- Saving wealth for children is permissible. «كنزلهما و كان ابوهما صالحاً» But hoarding wealth without spending (in charity/infaq) is forbidden.

3- Protecting the property of orphans is obligatory. «لغلامین یتیمین»

4- The good deeds of the father have an effect on the lives of the children. «ابوهما صالحاً»

5- Khidr was a Prophet. «ما فعلته عن امرى» (I did not do it of my own accord).

6- Sometimes events have deep analysis and interpretation and are in the path of human growth. The goal of these periods was the growth of Moses. «أراد ربّك، رحمةً من ربّك»

7- One must speak courteously before Allah. Where Khidr speaks of defect and flaw, he attributes it to himself, «أردتُ أن اَعیبها» (I intended to make it defective), and where the scope of the work is Divine, he attributes it to Allah. «أراد ربّك أن یبلغا اشدّهما» (Your Lord intended that they reach their full maturity).

8- The will of Allah's chosen servants (Awliya) is the Will of Allah. «أردتُ، أردنا، أراد ربّك»

Ayah

وَيَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَن ذِى ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ ۖ قُلْ سَأَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْكُم مِّنْهُ ذِكْرًا (۸۳)
They question you concerning Dhul Qarnayn. Say, ‘I will relate to you an account of him.’ (83)

Key Points

The word qarn (قرن) has two meanings: one is a long period of time (era), and the other is the horn of an animal. Dhul-Qarnayn was named by this description either because He had a long reign, or because He braided two strands of His hair like two horns, or because there were two small horns on His helmet. It is also possible that the intent of qarnayn (قرنین) is the East and West of the world,(91) and since He gained dominion over the entire East and West of the world, He was named Dhul-Qarnayn. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: "Dhul-Qarnayn was not a prophet, but He was a righteous man whom Allah loved, and He enjoined piety upon His people. The people struck Him on one side of His head, and He disappeared for a while. After that, He returned to the people and repeated His call. The people struck Him on the other side of His head. Then the Imam said: There is also a person among you with these characteristics.(92) Therefore, He was known as Dhul-Qarnayn because of these two strikes." Tafsir Namuneh, considering the statements of several Greek historians, several passages from the Torah (Book of Isaiah, Chapter 46, numbers 28 and 11), and the discovery of the statue of Cyrus in the nineteenth century AD, which had a crown with two horns, mostly identifies Dhul-Qarnayn with Cyrus. However, the late Sha'rani, in the margin of Tafsir Abul-Futuh, considers Dhul-Qarnayn to be Alexander the Great (Macedonian), who was a student of Aristotle from the age of thirteen. And the author of Tafsir Al-Mizan considers Him to be Cyrus. In any case, regarding how long Dhul-Qarnayn ruled, what His real name was, how old He lived, whether He was human or an angel, a prophet or a righteous servant? Was He Alexander or Cyrus? Why is He named Dhul-Qarnayn? What were His capabilities? What was the extent of His dominion? What were the length, width, and specifications of the dam He built? When was it built? Is this dam the same as the Great Wall of China or not? Many statements have been made, and discussing the details is not very beneficial. One must pursue the goals, not the minor issues in which there is no growth.

(91) Just as the Arabs say: qarnay ash-shams (قرنى الشمس), meaning the two horns of the sun, which refers to the East and West of the world. (92) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kamal al-Din by Saduq.

Messages

1- The Prophets were the reference point for the people and the answerers of their questions. «یسئلونك عن ذى القرنین»

2- The religious leader and preacher must be familiar with history and important events. «یسئلونك...» The one who becomes the leader of the people is asked various questions, just as the question about the soul is raised in other verses.

3- We should not leave the people's appropriate and logical questions unanswered. «سأتلوا»

4- When answering questions, we should not reply hastily. «سأتلوا»

5- History and its recounting are valuable to the extent that they contain admonition and lessons. «سأتلوا علیكم منه ذكراً»

6- The questions of the people were the context for the revelation of some verses. «یسئلونك... قل...»

Ayahs

إِنَّا مَكَّنَّا لَهُۥ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ مِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ سَبَبًا (۸٤)
Indeed We had granted him power in the land and given him the means to all things. (84)
فَأَتْبَعَ سَبَبًا (۸٥)
So he directed a means. (85)

Key Points

In the supplication (Du'a al-Mashlool) that Imam Ali عليه السلام taught the young man from Balkh, we read: «یا من نَصر ذى القرنین على ملوك الجبابرة» (O God, Who granted victory to Dhul-Qarnayn over the tyrannical kings). And in a hadith, that Imam considers Dhul-Qarnayn to be someone who possessed the signs of kingship and prophethood, and had knowledge of everything so that he could distinguish truth from falsehood, and Allah made the cities and hearts submit to him. (93) Dhul-Qarnayn and Solomon (Sulayman) were two believers who ruled the earth, and Nebuchadnezzar (Bakht al-Nasr) and Nimrod were two disbelievers who ruled the earth. (94) The power that Allah grants to His chosen servants (such as Prophet Solomon, Joseph, and some believers) is for use in the way of Allah. The Quran says regarding the good servants of Allah: «الّذین ان مكنّاهم فى الارض اقاموا الصلوة...» (95) They are those who, if We establish them with power in the land, establish prayer, pay Zakat, and revive Amr bil Ma'ruf (enjoining good) and Nahy 'anil Munkar (forbidding evil). And He criticizes those who use their power unjustly, saying: «كم اهلكنا قبلهم من قرنٍ مكنّاهم فى الارض» (96) How many generations before them did We destroy, whom We had established in the land, because they misused their power and governance.

93) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 94) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 95) Hajj, 41. 96) An'am, 6.

Messages

  1. Allah is the Ruler over all existence and natural means, and He grants establishment and power to whomever He wills. «مَكنّا له» But proper utilization depends on the human being's good intention and sound management. «فاَتبَع سببا»

  2. Sometimes Allah places all kinds of resources at the disposal of certain individuals. «مكنّاله... آتیناه من كلّ شى ء سببا»

  3. The natural order is a system of cause and effect, and everything has a means (a cause) that must be recognized and pursued. «من كلّ شى ءٍ سبباً فاَتْبع سببا» The special servants of Allah must also pursue righteous goals through natural means and resources.

  4. More important than possessing resources and power is using them well. «آتیناه...سبباً فاَتْبع سبباً»

  5. Dhul-Qarnayn, besides having resources, also possessed the knowledge of how to utilize the means. «فاَتْبَع سببا»

Ayah

حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ مَغْرِبَ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَجَدَهَا تَغْرُبُ فِى عَيْنٍ حَمِئَةٍ وَوَجَدَ عِندَهَا قَوْمًا ۗ قُلْنَا يَـٰذَا ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ إِمَّآ أَن تُعَذِّبَ وَإِمَّآ أَن تَتَّخِذَ فِيهِمْ حُسْنًا (۸٦)
When he reached the place where the sun sets, he found it setting over a warm sea, and by it he found a people. We said, ‘O Dhul Qarnayn! You will either punish them, or treat them with kindness.’ (86)

Key Points

«حَمِئة» refers to black, foul-smelling mud, sludge, and hot clay.

Messages

1- The Prophets and Divine Saints would personally rise up and take action to solve problems and save people; they did not merely suffice with prayer. «بلغ مغرب الشمس»

2- Travel and exploration undertaken with knowledge and purpose is affirmed. «حتّى اذا بلغ ...»

3- Dhul-Qarnayn was a Prophet, because He was addressed by Allah. «قلنا یاذاالقرنین»

4- Sometimes, authority must be delegated to managers and leaders. «امّا أن تُعذّب وامّا...» (The Saints of God possess authority granted by the Lord.)

5- Disbelief (Kufr) in the West has a very long history. «وجد عندها قوماً... أن تعذّب»

6- Prophets should not distance themselves from the people. «تتّخذ فیهم حسناً»

Ayahs

قَالَ أَمَّا مَن ظَلَمَ فَسَوْفَ نُعَذِّبُهُۥ ثُمَّ يُرَدُّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ فَيُعَذِّبُهُۥ عَذَابًا نُّكْرًا (۸۷)
He said, ‘As for him who is a wrongdoer, we will punish him. Then he shall be returned to his Lord and He will punish him with a dire punishment. (87)
وَأَمَّا مَنْ ءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَلَهُۥ جَزَآءً ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ ۖ وَسَنَقُولُ لَهُۥ مِنْ أَمْرِنَا يُسْرًا (۸۸)
But as for him who has faith and acts righteously, he shall have the best reward, and we will assign him easy tasks under our command.’ (88)

Messages

1- Disbelief and polytheism are oppression. In contrast to faith, the term oppression is used instead of polytheism: «امّا مَن ظَلَم... امّا من آمن»

2- The Islamic government has the right to deal severely with polytheists. «مَن ظَلَم فسوف نُعذّبه»

3- Dhul-Qarnayn was hopeful for the repentance of the oppressors, and therefore he did not hasten their punishment. «مَن ظَلم فسوف نُعذّبه»

4- Worldly torment does not prevent the retribution of the Hereafter. «نُعذّبه، فیُعذّبه»

5- Worldly retribution is known and specified, but the torment of the Hereafter is unprecedented (or terrible). «عذاباً نُكرا»

6- The good and the bad must not be treated equally, whether from the position of an individual or a government. «امّا مَن ظلم ... امّا مَن آمن»

7- Dhul-Qarnayn was the executor of Divine commands. In the previous Ayah, he was advised to treat them kindly: «تتخّذ فیهم حسناً» and here He (Allah) stated: «فله جزاءً الحسنى»

8- Divine leaders must govern the people using easy laws. «سنقول له من أمرنا یُسراً»

9- Severe dealing with oppressors and leniency toward believers is the method of Divine leaders. «عذاباً نُكراً... أمرنا یُسراً»

10- The duty of the Divine leader and ruler is to implement justice, combat oppression, and simplify social regulations. «عذاباً نُكراً... أمرنا یُسرا»

11- Be quick in encouragement and reward, but do not rush retribution and torment. The letter sīn (س) in «سنقول» indicates acceleration, while the word sawfa (سوف) indicates delay.

Ayahs

ثُمَّ أَتْبَعَ سَبَبًا (۸۹)
Thereafter he directed another means. (89)
حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ مَطْلِعَ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَجَدَهَا تَطْلُعُ عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ لَّمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُم مِّن دُونِهَا سِتْرًا (۹٠)
When he reached the place where the sun rises, he found it rising on a people for whom We had not provided any shield against it. (90)
كَذَٰلِكَ وَقَدْ أَحَطْنَا بِمَا لَدَيْهِ خُبْرًا (۹۱)
So it was, and We were fully aware of whatever [means] he possessed. (91)

Key Points

Dhu al-Qarnayn, after a journey to the West and establishing a just religious system among the coastal dwellers, also undertook a journey to the East. The meaning of the statement that they had no shelter except the sun is that they lived a primitive life without facilities. According to Imam Sadiq علیه السلام: They knew neither how to build houses nor how to tailor. The sun shone upon them without obstruction, such that their faces had become black.(97)

97) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Righteous and committed individuals possess follow-up and perseverance. «ثمّ اَتْبَع سبباً»

2- Men of God, despite having all types of welfare facilities, move to save the deprived and spread justice. «ثمّ اتْبَع سبباً...»

3- Recounting the journeys of the men of God and the blessings resulting from them is valuable and a source of lesson and admonition. «اَتْبَع سبباً حتّى اذا...»

4- What is important is guiding and serving the people, whether it is in the East or in the West. «ثمّ اتْبع سبباً...»

5- Allah has complete knowledge of individuals and the special blessings He has bestowed upon them. «احطنا بما لدیه خُبراً» (Recounting the journeys of Dhu al-Qarnayn, his encounters, and his conversations with the people is an example of Allah's comprehensive knowledge.)

Ayahs

ثُمَّ أَتْبَعَ سَبَبًا (۹۲)
Thereafter he directed another means. (92)
حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ بَيْنَ ٱلسَّدَّيْنِ وَجَدَ مِن دُونِهِمَا قَوْمًا لَّا يَكَادُونَ يَفْقَهُونَ قَوْلًا (۹۳)
When he reached [the place] between the two barriers, he found between them a people who could hardly understand a word [of his language]. (93)

Key Points

Considering the next Ayah, in which the people recognized Dhul-Qarnayn for his perfection, power, compassion, and competence, and they identified insecurity as the most important issue, believed its solution lay in the hands of Dhul-Qarnayn, and spoke with him. Therefore, the meaning of the phrase «لایكادون یفقهون قولا» (they hardly understand a word) is that their culture was low (or underdeveloped). Just as we read in Ayah 78 of Surah An-Nisa: «فمالِ هؤلاء القوم لایكادون یفقهون حدیثاً» (What is the matter with these people that they hardly understand any statement?). Why do they not understand any speech or discourse? The intent of the Ayah is not a lack of familiarity with a specific language or dialect, but rather: Why do they not follow the correct path!? In this Surah, three stories are narrated, and in all three, there is movement and Hijrah (migration): The Hijrah of the Companions of the Cave, the Hijrah of Moses for meeting Khidr, and the Hijrah of Dhul-Qarnayn; the first Hijrah is for preserving faith, the second is for acquiring knowledge, and the third is for saving the deprived.

Messages

1- The laws governing nature must be discovered and applied. «ثمّ اَتْبع سبباً»

2- Every movement, goal, and journey requires a specific means. The phrase «اَتبَع سببا» is repeated for every movement and journey.

3- For the men of God, cessation of activity, leisure, and retirement have no meaning. «ثمّ اَتْبَعَ سبباً»

4- The leader of the people must also visit other regions and stay informed about the situation. «حتّى اذا بلغ ...»

5- Unless there is movement and exploration, needs and realities will not be recognized. «اذا بلغ ... وجد»

6- Serving the deprived is a value, whether they are cultured or not. «لایكادون یفقهون»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰذَا ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ إِنَّ يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ مُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَهَلْ نَجْعَلُ لَكَ خَرْجًا عَلَىٰٓ أَن تَجْعَلَ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَهُمْ سَدًّا (۹٤)
They said, ‘O Dhul Qarnayn! Indeed Gog and Magog are causing disaster in this land. Shall we pay you a tribute on condition that you build a barrier between them and us?’ (94)

Key Points

Kharaaj (خراج) is something that comes out of the earth, and kharj (خَرج) is what is spent from wealth. Commentators and historians, using existing evidence, say that the meaning of Ya’juj and Ma’juj (Gog and Magog) refers to the Mongol and Tatar tribes. In a narration from the Commander of the Faithful, Ali عليه السلام, we read: Dhul-Qarnayn found a people who told Him: The people of Ya’juj and Ma’juj are behind this mountain, and when the crops and fruits are harvested, they attack and plunder or destroy everything. Shall we set aside an annual expense for you so that you may build a barrier for us? (98)

98) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Wherever people feel a need, they will invest. «فهل نجعل لك خرجاً» (Shall we assign a tribute for you?) In Ayah 77 of this Surah, the people did not give a piece of bread to the two Prophets of Allah, but in this Ayah, they are willing to make a large investment.

2- The friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah) are the best people to establish security and solve the problems of the people. «یا ذا القرنین...تجعل بیننا... سدّاً» (O Dhul-Qarnayn... that you may make between us... a barrier.)

3- The need for security is greater than the need for housing and clothing. «نجعل لك خرجاً على أن تجعل... سدّاً» (We shall assign a tribute for you on condition that you make... a barrier.) (The people who had no shelter or clothing against the sun asked Dhul-Qarnayn for security, not covering.)

4- Security and comfort hold special importance in human societies. «تجعل بیننا وبینهم سدّاً» (You may make between us and them a barrier.) Therefore, the severest punishments are also reserved for those who disrupt security. (99)

5- Imprisoning and restricting criminals is permissible. «سدّاً» (a barrier.)

99) Referring to the verses that describe the punishment for the "corruptors in the land" (mufsideen fil-ardh).

Ayahs

قَالَ مَا مَكَّنِّى فِيهِ رَبِّى خَيْرٌ فَأَعِينُونِى بِقُوَّةٍ أَجْعَلْ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ رَدْمًا (۹٥)
He said, ‘What my Lord has furnished me is better. Yet help me with some strength, and I will make a bulwark between you and them. (95)
ءَاتُونِى زُبَرَ ٱلْحَدِيدِ ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا سَاوَىٰ بَيْنَ ٱلصَّدَفَيْنِ قَالَ ٱنفُخُوا۟ ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَعَلَهُۥ نَارًا قَالَ ءَاتُونِىٓ أُفْرِغْ عَلَيْهِ قِطْرًا (۹٦)
Bring me pieces of iron!’ When he had levelled up between the flanks, he said, ‘Blow!’ When he had turned it into fire, he said, ‘Bring me molten copper to pour over it.’ (96)
فَمَا ٱسْطَـٰعُوٓا۟ أَن يَظْهَرُوهُ وَمَا ٱسْتَطَـٰعُوا۟ لَهُۥ نَقْبًا (۹۷)
So they could neither scale it, nor could they make a hole in it. (97)

Key Points

«رَدم» refers to closing a gap, «صَدف» refers to the side or edge, «قِطر» refers to molten copper, and «زُبَر» refers to large pieces of iron. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Taqiyyah (dissimulation) is a barrier between you and the opponents that cannot be climbed over or pierced. (100)

100) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- From the perspective of the Divine Saints, all resources are from Allah. «ما مكّنى فیه ربّى»

2- The Prophets did not request money or material possessions from the people for their mission and guidance. (101) «ما مكّنى فیه ربّى خیر»

3- Public resources must be utilized for the benefit of the people themselves. «فاعینونى»

4- To achieve a goal, in addition to planning, request, and budget, public resolve and participation are also necessary. «اعینونى»

5- Sometimes, instead of financial aid, human resources must be called upon for assistance. «فاعینونى»

6- The combination of iron and copper (alloying) is an innovation of the Prophets. «آتونى زُبُرالحدید... قِطراً» (The importance of this combination has been scientifically proven today.)

7- Thoroughness and flawless execution of tasks are the methods of the Prophets. «فما اسطاعوا...» Dhul-Qarnayn’s barrier was so strong and large that they could neither destroy it nor pass over it. «أن یظهروه... له نَقْباً»

8- In solving social problems, the cooperation of the people who are affected by the hardship is necessary. (Because work enthusiasm increases, they appreciate the value of the efforts, and they do not slack off.) «فاعینونى... فما اسطاعوا...»

9- Capable leaders, with the help of science, art, dedication, and cooperation, produce the best works from the simplest individuals and tools. Therefore, if the manager is capable, simple workers can execute the most important projects. «فاعینونى... فما اسطاعوا...»

101) In Surah Ash-Shu'ara, it is narrated five times from five Prophets that they told the people: We do not ask you for any material reward. The Prophet of Islam also set the reward for his mission as love for the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام, the fruit of which—love and obedience to them—returns to the person himself.

Ayah

قَالَ هَـٰذَا رَحْمَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّى ۖ فَإِذَا جَآءَ وَعْدُ رَبِّى جَعَلَهُۥ دَكَّآءَ ۖ وَكَانَ وَعْدُ رَبِّى حَقًّا (۹۸)
He said, ‘This is a mercy from my Lord. But when the promise of my Lord is fulfilled, He will level it; and my Lord’s promise is true.’ (98)

Key Points

The barrier of Dhul-Qarnayn is neither the Great Wall of China nor the other cases that have been mentioned, because these were not built from iron and copper. In the Caucasus region, in the Darial Gorge, an iron barrier has been found, near which there is also a river named Saos (meaning Cyrus). It seems more acceptable that this is the same barrier. (102)

102) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Men of God consider their successes to be from the Mercy of Allah, and they never become arrogant. «رحمة من ربّى»

2- All efforts, changes, and transformations are for the nurturing and growth of human beings. «ربّى» is repeated three times in the Ayah.

3- Divine Mercy (Rahmah) and Lordship (Rububiyyah) are interconnected. «رحمة من ربّى»

4- Establishing security is a Divine Mercy. «رحمة من ربّى» The construction of the barrier was for security.

5- The sacredness of work and effort, solid construction, public cooperation, management and industry, and lofty goals are all a collection of Divine favors. «هذا رحمة من ربّى»

6- By providing security for the people, one can better engage in propagation (Tabligh) and guidance (Irshad), and the groundwork for other tasks is laid. «رحمة ربّى... وعد ربّى حقّا» Dhul-Qarnayn introduced the people to the Origin (Mabda') and the Return (Ma'ad) after completing the barrier.

7- On the verge of the Day of Judgment, everything will be destroyed. «جعله دكّاء»

8- The strongest human fortresses are unstable before the Will and Power of Allah the Almighty. «جعله دكّاء»

9- Men of God make predictions relying on the favor of Allah. «فاذا جاء وعد ربّى جعله دكّاء»

10- Solid construction must be learned from the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah); they build a barrier that remains standing until the verge of the Day of Judgment. «فاذا جاء وعد ربّى جعله دكّاء»

11- One must both carry out worldly tasks flawlessly and firmly, and remember the Divine Power and the futility of one's own power. «فمااستطاعوا... كان وعد ربّى حقّاً»

12- Divine leaders both develop the worldly affairs of the people and remind them of their Hereafter. «فمااستطاعوا... كان وعد ربّى حقّاً»

Ayahs

۞ وَتَرَكْنَا بَعْضَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَمُوجُ فِى بَعْضٍ ۖ وَنُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَجَمَعْنَـٰهُمْ جَمْعًا (۹۹)
That day We shall let them surge over one another, the Trumpet will be blown, and We shall gather them all, (99)
وَعَرَضْنَا جَهَنَّمَ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْكَـٰفِرِينَ عَرْضًا (۱٠٠)
and on that day We shall bring hell into view visibly for the faithless. (100)
ٱلَّذِينَ كَانَتْ أَعْيُنُهُمْ فِى غِطَآءٍ عَن ذِكْرِى وَكَانُوا۟ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ سَمْعًا (۱٠۱)
—Those whose eyes were blind to My remembrance and who could not hear. (101)

Key Points

If we place the Ayah "وتركنا..." (And We left...) alongside the Ayah "حتّى اذا فُتِحَت یأجوجُ و مأجوجُ و هم من كلّ حدبٍ ینسلون و اقترب الوعد..." (Until when Gog and Magog are let loose, and they swiftly swarm from every elevation, and the Promise draws near...) (103), it seems that the issue of the people of Gog and Magog relates to the Day of Judgment and is apparently a prelude to it. Meaning, before the Day of Judgment, the people of Gog and Magog will be freed, released, and spread out like a wave. It is narrated from Imam Reza عليه السلام regarding the interpretation of the Ayah "اعینهم فى غِطاء عن ذِكرِى" (their eyes were under a covering from My remembrance) that He told Ma'mun: The meaning of "ذِكر" (remembrance) in this Ayah is Ali ibn Abi Talib عليهما السلام. (104) The meaning of the eye and ear in Ayah 101 is knowledge, insight, and recognition—that is, the eye and ear of the heart, not a specific organ in the head. Because the Quran sometimes attributes blindness to the heart and says: "مَن كان فى هذه اَعمى" (Whoever is blind in this [world]). (105)

103) Al-Anbiya, 96. 104) Tafsir Lahiji. 105) Al-Isra, 72.

Messages

1- Wherever there is security, it is due to His grace; if He were to abandon people for a moment, the world would become insecure. "تَركنا بعضهم..."

2- The breaking of the barrier of Dhul-Qarnayn is among the signs of the Day of Judgment. "اذا جاء وعد ربّى... نُفِخ فى الصّور"

3- Before the Day of Judgment, the earth will be plunged into chaos by certain peoples. "تركنا... یَموج" (We left... surging). Although this chaos will be rectified with the advent of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام.

4- The path to recognition is primarily through the eye and the ear. Their eyes were veiled, and their ears lacked the power to hear the truth. They did not understand the truth, otherwise, remembrance (ذِكر) is not something visible! "اعینهم فى غطاء عن ذِكرى"

5- A person who can hear, due to obstinacy, reaches a point where he is unable to hear the truth. "لایستطیعون سمعاً"

6- The reason for presenting Hell to the disbelievers is their own actions and hardness of heart in this world. "عَرضنا جهنّم...الذّین كانت ..."

Ayah

أَفَحَسِبَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَن يَتَّخِذُوا۟ عِبَادِى مِن دُونِىٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ ۚ إِنَّآ أَعْتَدْنَا جَهَنَّمَ لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ نُزُلًا (۱٠۲)
Do the faithless suppose that they have taken My servants for guardians in My stead? Indeed, We have prepared hell for the hospitality of the faithless. (102)

Key Points

In these verses, the portrait of the disbelievers is drawn as follows: A: Blindness of heart, «كانت اعینهم فى غطاء» B: Worshipping servants instead of worshipping Allah, «یتخّذوا عبادى من دونى اولیاء» C: Ignorance and fanaticism, «یحسبون أنّهم یُحسنون صنعاً»(106) The word «نُزُل» (Nuzul) either means a dwelling place or the first thing with which a guest is entertained.

106) Kahf, 104.

Messages

1- Polytheism (Shirk) and the beliefs of the polytheists are nothing more than imagination. «أفحسب»

2- Whoever is other than Allah, «دون اللّه», is a creature and servant of Allah. «عبادى» How have they taken a creature in place of Allah?

3- Accepting the guardianship (wilayah) of anyone other than Allah is disbelief (kufr). «كفروا, من دونى اولیاء»

4- Hell (Dūzakh) is currently existent, and the Divine punishment is serious and certain. «انّا اعتدنا»

5- The recompense for disbelief (kufr) is Hell. «اعتدنا جهنّم للكافرین»

Ayahs

قُلْ هَلْ نُنَبِّئُكُم بِٱلْأَخْسَرِينَ أَعْمَـٰلًا (۱٠۳)
Say, ‘Shall we inform you who are the biggest losers in their works? (103)
ٱلَّذِينَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ يُحْسِنُونَ صُنْعًا (۱٠٤)
