Those whose efforts are misguided in the life of the world, while they suppose they are doing good.’ (104)

Key Points

In narrations (Riwāyāt), this Ayah has been interpreted as referring to those who deny the Wilāyah (guardianship) of Amīr al-Mu'minīn Ali عليه السلام (107) and those who procrastinate in performing Hajj (108). Imam Ali عليه السلام said: The manifestation (Miṣdāq) of the Ayah is the People of the Book who were initially upon the truth, then introduced innovations (Bid'ah) into the religion, believing that they were doing good deeds. He then said: "و ما اهل النهر منهم ببعید" (And the people of Nahrawān are not far removed from them). The Nahrawānians are no less [in error] than them. (109)

Imam Husayn عليه السلام says in the Du'ā of 'Arafah: O Allah, our good deeds are [like] bad deeds, and our truths are nothing more than mere claims.

Just as benefits vary and have degrees—double, multiple times, ten times: "ضعف، اضعاف، عشرة امثالها" (double, manifold, ten times their like)—and sometimes seven hundred times, "فى كلّ سنبلةٍ مأة حبّة" (in every ear a hundred grains) (110), and sometimes beyond imagination, "فلا تعلم نفسٌ ما اُخفى لهم" (no soul knows what has been hidden for them) (111)—so too do losses and harms have stages and differences:

A: Sometimes it is a bad transaction. "بئسما اشتروا" (Evil is that for which they sold themselves) (112) B: Sometimes it yields no profit. "فما ربحت تجارتهم" (So their transaction brought no profit) (113) C: Sometimes it is a loss. "اشتروا الضّلالة بالهدى" (They purchased error [in exchange] for guidance) (114) D: Sometimes it is being immersed in loss. "لفى خسر" (are surely in [utter] loss) (115) E: Sometimes it is a great and manifest loss. "خسراناً مبینا" (a manifest loss) (116) F: Sometimes it is loss in all dimensions. "بالأخسرین اعمالاً" (those who are the greatest losers in [their] deeds) (117)

The losers are several groups: A: A group that does not perform good deeds. B: A group that works for this world, not the Hereafter. C: A group that works but knows their work is incorrect. D: A group that is in loss but imagines that they are profiting.

The first three groups might, through repentance (Tawbah), think of reforming themselves and making amends. However, the fourth group, because they do not seek a remedy, are the worst of people. Examples include Christian monks who deprive themselves of the lawful pleasures of this world and are also criticized by the Prophets, or hypocritical ascetics (Zāhid-namāhā).

Those who do not hold correct beliefs and follow their false imaginings do not suffer loss in just one action; rather, they suffer loss in all their endeavors. This is because the criterion for the value of any deed is its correct motivation. The Quran has also criticized calculations based on baseless imagination and conjecture using expressions such as: "أحسب، یحسبون، لا یحسبنّ، أفحسبتم و..." (Do you think, they think, let them not think, did you then think, etc.).

107) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 108) Tafsir Abul Futuh. 109) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān. 110) Al-Baqarah, 261. 111) As-Sajdah, 17. 112) Al-Baqarah, 90. 113) Al-Baqarah, 16. 114) Al-Baqarah, 175. 115) Al-Asr, 2. 116) An-Nisā, 119. 117) Al-Kahf, 103.

Messages

1- The Prophet is commissioned to explain the real profit and loss of the people. "قل... اعمالا" (Say... deeds)

2- The method of question and answer is one of the best methods of teaching and training. "هل ..." (Shall We...?)

3- Man is susceptible to being heedless of his own profit and loss. "هل ننبّئكم..." (Shall We inform you...?)

4- No deed is erased, even if it appears to be lost. "ضلّ سعیهم" (their effort has been lost)

5- We should not rely on imagination; we must be realistic. "یحسبون" (they think)

Ayahs

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ وَلِقَآئِهِۦ فَحَبِطَتْ أَعْمَـٰلُهُمْ فَلَا نُقِيمُ لَهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ وَزْنًا (۱٠٥)
They are the ones who deny the signs of their Lord and encounter with Him. So their works have failed. On the Day of Resurrection We will not give them any weight. (105)
ذَٰلِكَ جَزَآؤُهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ بِمَا كَفَرُوا۟ وَٱتَّخَذُوٓا۟ ءَايَـٰتِى وَرُسُلِى هُزُوًا (۱٠٦)
That is their requital—hell—because of their unfaith and for deriding My signs and apostles. (106)

Key Points

| "Habaṭ" (حَبط), linguistically, refers to the swelling of an animal's belly due to eating poisonous plants, putting it at risk of death. Anyone who sees it thinks it is a healthy, well-fed animal, while its belly is full of gas and the animal itself is poisoned. The nullification (or ruin) of human deeds is also called habaṭ because those deeds have become poisoned, hollow, and exposed to destruction by sins. People on the Day of Judgment are in three groups: A: A group whose goodness is so great that they do not need the Scale (Mizan). B: A group whose evil and wretchedness is so great that they do not need the Scale. «فلا نُقیمُ لهم یوم القیامة وزنا» C: An intermediate group whose deeds must be calculated. «امّا مَن ثَقُلت موازینه. فهو فى عیشة راضیة. و امّا مَن خَفّت موازینه. فاُمّه هاویة»(118)

118) Al-Qari'ah, 6-9.

Messages

1- Disbelief in Allah causes the nullification of deeds, and whatever becomes hollow has no weight. «حبطت، فلا نُقیمُ لهم... وزناً»

2- Truth has weight with Allah, but falsehood is vain and weightless. «فلا نُقیم... وزناً»

3- Man's destiny is tied to his own deeds. «جزائهم... بما كفروا»

4- The ultimate consequence of disbelief and mocking the divine signs and Messengers is Hellfire. «بما كفروا و...»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ كَانَتْ لَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتُ ٱلْفِرْدَوْسِ نُزُلًا (۱٠۷)
As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, they shall have the gardens of Firdaws for abode, (107)
خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا لَا يَبْغُونَ عَنْهَا حِوَلًا (۱٠۸)
to remain in them [forever]; they will not seek to leave it for another place. (108)

Key Points

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Paradise has one hundred levels, the best of which is "Firdaws." Whenever you supplicate, ask Allah for Firdaws. (119)

119) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Faith (Iman) and righteous deeds (Amal Salih) are conditions for receiving the blessings of Paradise. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات كانت لهم...»

2- In education (tarbiyah), warning alongside glad tidings (basharat) and reward in contrast to punishment (adhāb) are necessary. In the previous few verses, the means of hospitality for the disbelievers was stated as Hell: «اعتدنا جهنّم للكافرین نُزلاً» and here, Firdaws is the means of hospitality for the believers. «كانت لهم جنّات الفردوس نزلاً»

3- If a person resides in any place for a long period, he becomes tired, and if he sees a better place, he wishes to move there. However, despite the eternity of Paradise, the inhabitants of Paradise will never wish to move away from it. «لا یبغون عنها حولاً»

Ayah

قُل لَّوْ كَانَ ٱلْبَحْرُ مِدَادًا لِّكَلِمَـٰتِ رَبِّى لَنَفِدَ ٱلْبَحْرُ قَبْلَ أَن تَنفَدَ كَلِمَـٰتُ رَبِّى وَلَوْ جِئْنَا بِمِثْلِهِۦ مَدَدًا (۱٠۹)
Say, ‘If the sea were ink for the words of my Lord, the sea would be spent before the words of my Lord are finished, though We replenish it with another like it.’ (109)

Key Points

«مداد» refers to the ink poured into the inkwell, which becomes the means for drawing the pen across the paper and assists the pen in writing. «كلمات ربّ» (the Words of the Lord) are either His promises and creations, or wisdoms and concepts, or revelation and everything that bears a sign of God. Therefore, every particle, every atom, and every cell is one of the Words of the Lord.

Messages

1- Divine signs are infinite, and man is incapable even of counting or writing them down. «لنفد البحر قبل ان تنفد»

2- Every phenomenon is a sign and a Word from the Lord, and it is in the path of human growth and development. «كلمات ربّى»

Ayah

قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَىَّ أَنَّمَآ إِلَـٰهُكُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ ۖ فَمَن كَانَ يَرْجُوا۟ لِقَآءَ رَبِّهِۦ فَلْيَعْمَلْ عَمَلًا صَـٰلِحًا وَلَا يُشْرِكْ بِعِبَادَةِ رَبِّهِۦٓ أَحَدًۢا (۱۱٠)
Say, ‘I am just a human being like you. It has been revealed to me that your God is the One God. So whoever expects to encounter his Lord—let him act righteously, and not associate anyone with the worship of his Lord.’ (110)

Key Points

This Ayah contains Monotheism (Tawhid), "الهٌ واحد" (Your God is one God); Prophethood (Nubuwwah), "یُوحى الىّ" (It is revealed to me); the Resurrection (Ma'ad), "لقاء ربّه" (the meeting with his Lord); hope in Divine Mercy, "یرجوا" (hopes); effort to attain it, "فلیَعمل" (let him do); and sincerity in action, "لایُشرك" (let him not associate). For this reason, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: If only the last Ayah of Surah Al-Kahf had been revealed to my Ummah, it would have been sufficient for them. (120)

In many narrations concerning this Ayah, the importance of sincerity (Ikhlas) and the danger of ostentation (Riya) and subtle polytheism (Shirk Khafi) have been discussed. Imam Reza عليه السلام saw Ma'mun performing Wudu (ablution) while his servant was pouring water onto his hands. The Imam, by reciting the phrase "ولا یشرك بعبادة ربّه احداً" (nor associate anyone in the worship of his Lord), made him understand that seeking help in Wudu and acts of worship is a form of Shirk (polytheism) and invalidates the act. (121)

It is mentioned in a Hadith: Whoever recites this Ayah when going to sleep will wake up at whatever hour he desires. (122)

120) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur. 121) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan; However, in the book Usul al-Kafi (Vol. 3, p. 69), the issue of seeking help in Wudu is raised concerning Imam Reza عليه السلام Himself, where the Imam did not allow anyone to pour water onto His blessed hands. 122) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- We should not introduce ourselves as more than what we are; the Prophets considered themselves human. "أنا بشر مثلكم" (I am only a human being like you).

2- The condition for prophethood is being human, so that the Prophet can also be a practical role model for other human beings. "أنا بشر مثلكم"

3- Divine leaders must prevent exaggeration and excess (ghuluww). "أنا بشر مثلكم"

4- For performing a godly deed, even hope for Divine reward is sufficient, even if there is no certainty. "فمن كان یرجوا... فلیعمل" (So whoever hopes... let him do).

5- Hope in a person must be a permanent and continuous state, not momentary. "فمن كان یرجوا" (So whoever hopes).

6- Hope without action is ineffective. "یرجوا, فلیعمل" (hopes, let him do).

7- Death is certain for everyone, but what is more valuable is that a person desires the meeting with Allah. "یرجوا لقاء ربّه" (hopes for the meeting with his Lord).

8- Shirk (polytheism), in whatever form it takes, is forbidden. "لایشرك بعبادة ربّه احداً" (nor associate anyone in the worship of his Lord).

9- The value of deeds lies in three aspects: the deed itself, the doer of the deed, and the intention and goal of the deed. All three aspects are mentioned in this Ayah. "عملاً صالحاً" (righteous deed), "من كان یرجوا" (whoever hopes), and "و لایشرك بعبادة ربّه" (nor associate anyone in the worship of his Lord).

10- This Ayah expresses both Monotheism in Divinity (Tawhid al-Uluhiyyah), "الهكم اله واحد" (Your God is one God), and Monotheism in Lordship and Worship (Tawhid al-Rububiyyah and Tawhid al-Ibadah). "لا یشرك بعبادة ربّه احداً" (nor associate anyone in the worship of his Lord).
